You must include a prominent statement that the Modification is derived, directly or indirectly, from Original Code provided by the Initial Developer and including the name of the Initial Developer in (a) the Source Code, and (b) in any notice in an Executable version or related documentation in which You describe the origin or ownership of the Covered Code.

You must make sure that they, too, receive or can get the source code. If you link other code with the library, you must provide complete object files to the recipients, so that they can relink them with the library after making changes to the library and recompiling it. And you must show them these terms so they know their rights.
    
    We protect your rights with a two-step method: (1) we copyright the library, and (2) we offer you this license, which gives you legal permission to copy, distribute and/or modify the library.
    
    To protect each distributor, we want to make it very clear that there is no warranty for the free library. Also, if the library is modified by someone else and passed on, the recipients should know that what they have is not the original version, so that the original author's reputation will not be affected by problems that might be introduced by others.
    
    Finally, software patents pose a constant threat to the existence of any free program. We wish to make sure that a company cannot effectively restrict the users of a free program by obtaining a restrictive license from a patent holder. Therefore, we insist that any patent license obtained for a version of the library must be consistent with the full freedom of use specified in this license.
    
    Most GNU software, including some libraries, is covered by the ordinary GNU General Public License. This license, the GNU Lesser General Public License, applies to certain designated libraries, and is quite different from the ordinary General Public License. We use this license for certain libraries in order to permit linking those libraries into non-free programs.
    
    When a program is linked with a library, whether statically or using a shared library, the combination of the two is legally speaking a combined work, a derivative of the original library. The ordinary General Public License therefore permits such linking only if the entire combination fits its criteria of freedom. The Lesser General Public License permits more lax criteria for linking other code with the library.
    
    We call this license the "Lesser" General Public License because it does Less to protect the user's freedom than the ordinary General Public License. It also provides other free software developers Less of an advantage over competing non-free programs. These disadvantages are the reason we use the ordinary General Public License for many libraries. However, the Lesser license provides advantages in certain special circumstances.
    
    For example, on rare occasions, there may be a special need to encourage the widest possible use of a certain library, so that it becomes a de-facto standard. To achieve this, non-free programs must be allowed to use the library. A more frequent case is that a free library does the same job as widely used non-free libraries. In this case, there is little to gain by limiting the free library to free software only, so we use the Lesser General Public License.
    
    In other cases, permission to use a particular library in non-free programs enables a greater number of people to use a large body of free software. For example, permission to use the GNU C Library in non-free programs enables many more people to use the whole GNU operating system, as well as its variant, the GNU/Linux operating system.
    
    Although the Lesser General Public License is Less protective of the users' freedom, it does ensure that the user of a program that is linked with the Library has the freedom and the wherewithal to run that program using a modified version of the Library.

    agreement terms follows:
    
    Limitation of Liability: Except for breach of the warranties made by

    aircraft navigation/control or "life critical" applications (which may
    directly or indirectly result in physical injury or loss of life).

    claim alleging that the software or the combination of the Software with

    secret or other proprietary right. However, in no event is SSHTools,

    solely and directly from (i) unauthorized modifications of the Software;
    or (ii) unauthorized combination or use of the Software with products
    not contemplated by the Agreement if such infringement would not have
    arisen but for such combination or use.

    at its expense and option, will procure the right to continue to use it,
    replace it with non-infringing material with similar functionality or
    modify the infringing portion to be non-infringing without detracting
    from performance or functionality.

    source code escrow provisions.  From Sun's records, if Oracle received
    the source code, then SSHTools would require prohibitions that the code
    will not be distributed with the Java Development Kit (JDK) or Java
    Runtime Environment (JRE).
    
    Open Source Licenses/No Certification of Software: SSHTools is not
    required to provide a Certification of [Third Part] Software and does
    not provide any warranties for the completeness/accuracy of such
    Certification. However, SSHTools has provided a list of copyright
    notices and third party which are presumably applicable to the version
    of the Software provided at the time the Agreement was signed. [This is\
    as described in the "Additional Components" and "Attribution\
    requirements" sections in the new summary above.]
    
    No Obligation to Comply with Oracle Security Standards:  SSHTools is not
    required to comply with Oracle's Security Standards. Development has
    confirmed that SSHTools will not be provided any Oracle customer
    information, even as part of support.
    
    No Security Questionnaire:  SSHTools is not required to provide a
    completed Security Questionnaire and does not provide any warranties for
    the completeness/accuracy of such Certification. Therefore, Development
    needs to either complete one itself or obtain one from SSHTools (or
    otherwise receive approval from Oracle's product security for the lack
    of a Questionnaire).  One may already exist since the Software is used
    in other Oracle products.
    
    Support:  At Oracle's option and subject to payment of the annual
    support fee, Oracle may elect to receive annual support.  Support is
    limited and solely consists of:  (a) access to updates and upgrades; (b)
    email or telephone based access to SSHTools support team from 9:00 to
    23:00 Greenwich Mean Time; (c) 24 hour response for non-critical
    requests; (d) 8 hour response time for critical requests ("critical
    requests" are requests determined by Oracle to be "critical" and
    Oracle's customer's system's functionality is impaired or has limited
    availability or use).
    
    Confidentiality:
    
    Neither party is liable for any inadvertent or unauthorized disclosure
    of Confidential Information; provided it took reasonable care to prevent
    disclosure and then to mitigate any damage and prevent further
    disclosure.  Development has confirmed that Oracle will not disclose any
    Oracle sensitive information since there is little recourse for
    disclosure by SSHTools.
    
    Otherwise, fairly consistent with Oracle's standard confidentiality terms:
    
    i.      SSHTools Confidential Information: The source code for the Software
    
    ii. Oracle Confidential Information: Source code of the Oracle
    Products and any information marked to identified as confidential.
    
    iii.      Each party must protect the other's Confidential Information
    with the same protection it provides its own similar information (and at
    least to a degree of reasonable care).
    
    iv. Neither party has an obligation to protect the following: (A)
    generally known information; (B) received by the receiving party without
    an obligation of confidentiality; (C) is rightfully obtained without
    obligation of confidentiality; (D) is independently developed without
    breach of the Agreement.
    
    v.      If disclosure is legally compelled, the receiving party must
    give the original disclosing party prompt notice and use reasonable
    efforts to cooperating in obtaining a protective order (and, if
    applicable, respond to any SEC comments in order to assist in obtaining
    confidential protection).
    
    vi.      Subject to the other party's copyrights and patents, either
    party may create or acquire similar products and both parties may use
    the any Residual Knowledge related to the other party's Confidential
    Information. Residual Knowledge is defined "information which may be
    retained in the unaided memory of the receiving party's employees and
    contractors who had access to the other party's Confidential
    Information".  These rights are consistent with Oracle's standard terms
    but Residual Knowledge is more broadly defined in Oracle'standard
    inbound license (TLA). However, Development has confirmed that Oracle
    will not disclose any Oracle sensitive information since SSHTools may
    use any Residual Knowledge associated with such Oracle information.
    
    IP Ownership: SSHTools retain ownership of all intellectual property
    rights in the Software and considers the Software to be valuable trade
    secrets.  Oracle retains all rights to the Oracle Products and any
    modifications to, and derivative works of, the Software made by or on
    behalf of Oracle.
    
    Warranties:  SSHTools provides the following warranties:
    
    SSHTools warrants that the Software and its trademarks and copyrights do
    not infringe or violate any third patent, copyright or other proprietary
    right.
    
    That SSHTools is not aware of any facts upon which a claim for
    infringement could be based.
    
    SSHTools warrants that the Software does not include any viruses or
    other disrupting, damagining, corrupting or interfering code.
    
    SSHTools does not provide performance warranties or any other warranties.
    
    License Metric: The broad license accommodates all license metrics.
    
    Fee Structure: One-time fully-paid up license fee of $39,600 already
    paid by Sun. Annual support is available at Oracle's election for an
    annual fee of $9,900 which Oracle has continued to pay for use of
    support of J2SSH Maverick used in other Oracle products. Fees are due
    45 days from receipt of inventory.
    
    Term:  The Agreement has a 7 year term expiring in 2015.  Oracle may
    terminate at any time upon written notice or if SSHTools become
    insolvent, goes bankrupt or ceases to conduct business. Either party may
    terminate for uncured breaches.  The license grant survives termination
    of the Agreement.
    
    Continued Rights:  No continued rights provisions.  However, the license
    is perpetual and irrevocable and survives termination of the Agreement
    so no continued rights for the license grants should be needed.  The
    only possibly concern for Oracle would be no post-termination support.
    Development has confirmed is an acceptable risk in this situation since
    alternative technology for this functionality would be open source
    without any support.
    
    Governing Law:  New York
    
    Assignment:  Neither party may assign the Agreement without the other
    party's approval except that Oracle may assign it to an Affiliate or a
    successor-in-interest without SSHTool's approval.

Documentation Accessibility
---------------------------

For information about Oracle's commitment to accessibility, visit the Oracle Accessibility Program website at [http://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=acc&id=docacc](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=acc&id=docacc)
.

Access to Oracle Support

Oracle customers that have purchased support have access to electronic support through My Oracle Support. For information, visit [http://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=acc&id=info](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=acc&id=info)
 or visit [http://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=acc&id=trs](https://docs.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=acc&id=trs)
 if you are hearing impaired.

# OCI Kubernetes Engine (OKE) support for 20,000 worker nodes per Kubernetes cluster

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)
 / Release Notes

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/releasenotes/conteng/conteng_20000-node-support.htm#dcoc-content-body)

OCI Kubernetes Engine (OKE) support for 20,000 worker nodes per Kubernetes cluster
==================================================================================

*   **Services:** [Kubernetes Engine](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/conteng/index.htm)
    
*   **Release Date:** July 08, 2026

You can now use OKE to create enhanced clusters with up to 20,000 managed (or self-managed) nodes per cluster, with Oracle approval. Contact your Oracle account team for approval if you plan to use clusters with more than 5,000 worker nodes.

This increased limit applies to enhanced clusters that use the flannel CNI plugin for pod networking, subject to regional and realm capacity availability.

Enhanced clusters that use the VCN-Native Pod Networking CNI plugin have a default limit of 2,000 managed or self-managed nodes per cluster. If you need more worker nodes, contact your Oracle account team to review the use case, requirements, and availability.

With more worker nodes in a single cluster, you can deploy larger workloads on that cluster to improve resource utilization and reduce operational overhead. You also have fewer, larger environments to operate, secure, monitor, upgrade, and manage.

For more information, see [Container Engine for Kubernetes Limits](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/General/Concepts/servicelimits.htm#Container_Engine_for_Kubernetes_Limits)
 and [Planning for Enhanced Clusters Up to 20,000 Nodes](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengplanningfor20knodes.htm)
.

---

Resource Manager adds premium jobs for higher concurrent job limits
===================================================================

*   **Services:** [Resource Manager](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/resource-manager/index.htm)
    
*   **Release Date:** July 07, 2026

Resource Manager now supports premium jobs for tenancies that need to run more concurrent jobs. It provides customers with a seamless user experience. For more information, see [Enabling Premium Jobs](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/ResourceManager/Concepts/premium-jobs.htm)
.

# Language

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/language/using/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Language
========

Language offers REST API for powerful text analytics and translations. Analyze vast amounts of unstructured text with ease, use pretrained and custom models. Perform sentiment analysis, key phrase extraction, text classification, named entity recognition and detect PII data in text. Tailor models for domain-specific tasks and effortlessly translate text across various languages. Language also supports document translation and asynchronous jobs for efficiently processing large-volume workloads.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/language/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about the Language service](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/language/using/overview.htm "Language is server-less and multi-tenant service that's accessible using REST API calls. With pretrained and custom models, you can process unstructured text and extract insights without data science expertise.")

[Try Language with the LiveLabs workshop](https://apexapps.oracle.com/pls/apex/dbpm/r/livelabs/view-workshop?wid=887)

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/artificial-intelligence/language/)

[Create IAM policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/language/using/policies.htm "Learn about the resource policies including API permissions.")

#### Developer tools

[Language API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/language/latest/)

[Language CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/ai/language.html)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

#### Translation

[Asynchronous Document Translation](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/language/using/translate-document.htm "The OCI Language service Asynchronous Document Translation model translates text into a chosen language")

#### Pretrained Models

[Analyzing text](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/language/using/analyze_text.htm "Use the pretrained models to analyze text and retrieve the results using the Language service.")

[Translating text](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/language/using/translate-text.htm#console)

#### Async Jobs

[Creating Async Translation Jobs with Pretrained Models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/language/using/async-job-translation.htm "Create an Async translation job with Language pretrained models.")

#### Community

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://community.oracle.com/tech/apps-infra/categories/18430-cloud-infrastructure)

[AI solutions hub](https://www.oracle.com/artificial-intelligence/solutions/)

#### Custom Models

[Learn about custom models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/language/using/custom-models-about.htm "Creating a custom model typically involves five steps.")

[Create policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/language/using/policies-custom-models.htm "Before using Language for custom model training, have your tenancy administrator set up the following policies:")

[Datasets](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/language/using/allowed-datasets.htm "Review the supported datasets for custom models, and how to convert datasets into a supported format.")

[Label Studio Integration](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/language/using/label-studio.htm "Oracle's Data Labeling Service is being deprecated. We recommend you migrate your labeled datasets to Label Studio, an open source and marketplace-supported labeling tool.")
  **New**

[Create and train models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/language/using/create-train-model.htm "Use a Language project to contain a model that describes the labelled data.")

#### Healthcare Models

[Healthcare Models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/language/using/healthcare-models.htm "Learn about Language service healthcare models.")

[Healthcare NLP Models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/language/using/healthcare-nlp-models.htm "Learn about the Language service Healthcare NLP models to extract entities from healthcare records such as electronic health records (EHR), progress notes, and clinical trial documents.")

# Data Labeling

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-labeling/using/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Data Labeling
=============

Data Labeling is the process of identifying properties of documents, text, and images, and annotating them with those properties. The topic of a news article, the sentiment of a tweet, the caption of an image, important words spoken in an audio recording, the genre of a video are all examples of a data label.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/data-labeling/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about data labeling](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-labeling/using/about.htm "Find out about Data Labeling, what it is and how to use it.")

[Learn about datasets](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-labeling/using/datasets.htm "Learn about datasets and how you create and use them in Data Labeling.")

[Learn about work requests](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-labeling/using/work-requests.htm "Work requests are long-running operations on a dataset started by Data Labeling.")

[Data Labeling overview video](https://apexapps.oracle.com/pls/apex/f?p=44785:265:0:::265:P265_CONTENT_ID:31668)

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/artificial-intelligence/data-labeling/)

#### Developer Tools

[Data Labeling API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/datalabeling-dp/latest/)

[OCI SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

[Data Labeling CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/3.2.0/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/data-labeling-service.html)

[Oracle Accelerated Data Science (ADS) SDK](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/ads-sdk/latest/user_guide/data_labeling/index.html)

[Data Labeling samples in Github](https://github.com/oracle-samples/oci-data-science-ai-samples/tree/master/data_labeling_examples)

#### Prerequisites

[Before you begin](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-labeling/using/getting-started.htm#before-you-begin "Find out what you need in place before you can start using Data Labeling.")

[Create buckets](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-labeling/using/set-up-admin.htm#set-up-object-store "You must create buckets in a compartment in Object Storage before you can upload documents, images or text to Object Storage for use with Data Labeling.")

[Create IAM policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-labeling/using/set-up-identity-policies.htm "Data Labeling requires policies to be set in IAM to access resources to manage datasets and label records.")

#### Community

[Troubleshooting Data Labeling](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/data-labeling/using/troubleshooting.htm "Use troubleshooting information to identify and address common issues that can occur while working with Data Labeling.")

Application Dependency Management
=================================

Application Dependency Management is a developer service in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure that detects security vulnerabilities in application dependencies.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/adm/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about Application Dependency Management](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/concepts/adm_overview.htm "Application Dependency Management (ADM) detects security vulnerabilities in application dependencies.")

[Create IAM Policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/concepts/policies.htm "You use the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Identity and Access Management (IAM) service to create policies.")

#### Remediation Recipes

[List Remediation Recipes](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/list_remediation_recipes.htm "List remediation recipes.")

[Create Remediation Recipe](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/create-remediation-recipe.htm "Create a remediation recipe.")

[Delete Remediation Recipe](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/deleting_remediation_recipe.htm "When you no longer require a remediation recipe, you can delete it.")

#### Remediation Runs

[List Remediation Runs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/list_run.htm "View the list of remediation runs in the selected compartment. For every remediation run, you can view the last stage details, status, and associated remediation recipe.")

[Create Remediation Run](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/create_remediation_run.htm "Running a remediation recipe.")

[Delete Remediation Run](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/delete_run.htm "Delete a remediation run from your tenancy.")

[Cancel Remediation Run](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/cancel_run.htm "Cancel a remediation run which is in progress.")

#### Knowledge Bases

[Delete Knowledge Base](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/KnowledgeBases/delete-knowledge-base.htm "Delete a knowledge base using the Console, CLI, or API.")

#### Developer Tools

[CLI for Application Dependency Management](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/adm.html)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

#### Support

[Troubleshooting](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/troubleshooting-adm.htm "Use troubleshooting information to identify and address common issues that can occur while working with ADM.")

[Support Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

#### Vulnerability Audits

[List Vulnerability Audits](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/VulnerabilityAudits/list-vulnerability-audit.htm "Describes how to list the vulnerability audits in your tenancy.")

[Create Vulnerability Audit](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/application-dependency-management/tasks/VulnerabilityAudits/create-vulnerability-audit.htm "Describes how to create a vulnerability audit.")

# Document Understanding

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/document-understanding/using/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Document Understanding
======================

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Document Understanding is an AI service that lets developers extract text, tables, and other key data from document files through APIs and CLI tools. With Document Understanding, you can automate tedious business processing tasks with prebuilt AI models, and customize document extraction to fit your industry-specific needs.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/document-understanding/index.htm)

#### Get Started

[Service Overview](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/document-understanding/using/overview.htm "Document Understanding is a serverless, multitenant service, that you can use to detect and classify text, tables, and other key data from document files that you upload.")

[Getting Started](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/document-understanding/using/getting_started.htm "Learn how to get started with using Document Understanding.")

[IAM Policies for User Permission in Document Understanding](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/document-understanding/using/about_document-understanding_policies.htm "Learn about Document Understanding's resource policies including API permissions.")

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/artificial-intelligence/document-understanding/)

#### Concepts

[Pretrained Document AI Models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/document-understanding/using/pretrained_doc_ai_models.htm "Document Understanding provides pretrained models that let you organize and extract text and structure from business documents.")

[Custom Generative Models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/document-understanding/using/custom-kv-generative-extraction.htm "The OCI Document Service features Key-Value Extraction powered by Large Multimodal Models (LMMs), offering enhanced accuracy and adaptability over conventional model-driven methods.")
  **New**

[Custom Classic Models](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/document-understanding/using/custom-models-about.htm "In Document Understanding, you can leverage custom models for extracting key-value pairs and classifying documents, tailoring the process to your specific needs.")

# Support for Kubernetes version 1.36.1

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)
 / Release Notes

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/releasenotes/conteng/conteng-K8s-1-36-1-support.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Support for Kubernetes version 1.36.1
=====================================

*   **Services:** [Kubernetes Engine](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/conteng/index.htm)
    
*   **Release Date:** July 07, 2026

OCI Kubernetes Engine (OKE) now supports Kubernetes version 1.36.1, in addition to versions 1.35.2 and 1.34.2. With the availability of support for Kubernetes version 1.36.1, OKE will cease support for Kubernetes version 1.33.10 on August 10, 2026. Oracle strongly recommends you immediately upgrade clusters to Kubernetes version 1.34.2, 1.35.2, or 1.36.1. For more information about Kubernetes version 1.36.1, see the [Kubernetes Changelog](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/CHANGELOG/CHANGELOG-1.36.md#v1361)
.

Note the following:

*   Although Kubernetes version 1.33.10 will not be supported after August 10, 2026, it might continue to be available for selection. However, Oracle strongly recommends you upgrade clusters to Kubernetes version 1.34.2, 1.35.2, or 1.36.1.
*   New clusters that you create with OKE to run Kubernetes version 1.36.1 (or later) support only the Kubernetes Key Management Service (KMS) v2 provider. Existing clusters that you upgrade to Kubernetes version 1.36.1 (or later) support both KMS v1 and KMS v2 to preserve compatibility with existing KMS v1 configurations.
    
*   For worker nodes in clusters running Kubernetes version 1.35 (and later), Kubernetes Engine supports OKE OL8 images, and custom images based on OKE OL8 images. Custom images based on OL8 platform images are also supported, but only if you enable cgroups v2 (see [Enabling Cgroups v2 on OL8 Worker Nodes Using Custom Images](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengcreatingcgroupv2enabledworkernodes.htm)
    ).
    
    In clusters running Kubernetes version 1.35 (and later), the use of platform images (both OL7 platform images and OL8 platform images) is not recommended. We strongly recommend the use of OKE images for all new deployments and upgrades. If you want to continue to use a platform image, create a custom image based on the platform image and specify the custom image's OCID when creating or updating a node pool (note that we do not recommend this approach).
    
    For more information, see [Notes about Kubernetes version 1.35 (and later) and Support for OKE Images, Platform Images, and Custom Images](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/ContEng/Reference/contengimagesshapes.htm#contengimagesshapes_topic-k8s-135-support)
    .
    

To upgrade a cluster to a new Kubernetes minor version:

1.  Upgrade the Kubernetes version running on the control plane by following the instructions in [Upgrading the Kubernetes Version on Control Plane Nodes in a Cluster](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengupgradingk8smasternode.htm)
    .
2.  Having upgraded the control plane, upgrade the Kubernetes version running on worker nodes in a node pool by following the instructions in [Upgrading the Kubernetes Version on Worker Nodes in a Cluster](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/ContEng/Tasks/contengupgradingworkernodes.htm)
    .

**Note:**

Always follow the [Kubernetes skew policy](https://kubernetes.io/docs/setup/release/version-skew-policy/#supported-version-skew)
 for Kubernetes minor version support. Note that in Kubernetes version 1.28, the Kubernetes skew policy expanded. Prior to Kubernetes version 1.28, the skew policy required a cluster's control plane nodes to be no more than two versions ahead of worker nodes. From Kubernetes version 1.28, the Kubernetes skew policy allows control plane nodes to be up to three versions ahead of worker nodes.

Example skew policy:

*   Kubernetes control plane nodes are at version 1.36.1
*   Kubernetes worker nodes must be at version 1.36.1, 1.35.2, 1.34.2, or 1.33.10

Note that although the worker nodes in a node pool must follow the [Kubernetes skew policy](https://kubernetes.io/docs/setup/release/version-skew-policy/#supported-version-skew)
, you do not have to upgrade them one minor version at a time.

For more information, see [Upgrading Clusters to Newer Kubernetes Versions](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/ContEng/Concepts/contengaboutupgradingclusters.htm)
.

---

# HeatWave: Multi-Source Replication

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)
 / Release Notes

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/releasenotes/mysql-database/heatwave-multi-source-replication.htm#dcoc-content-body)

HeatWave: Multi-Source Replication
==================================

*   **Services:** [MySQL HeatWave](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/mysql-database/index.htm)
    
*   **Release Date:** July 08, 2026

Inbound replication now supports multi-source replication. A replica DB system can connect to up to 18 MySQL sources by using one replication channel for each source. For more information, see [Inbound Replication](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/mysql-database/doc/inbound-replication.html)
.

---

# Email Delivery

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Email Delivery
==============

Email Delivery is an email sending service and SMTP relay that provides a fast and reliable managed solution for sending both high volume bulk and transactional emails that need to reach the inbox.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/email/)

#### Getting Started

[Learn about Email Delivery service](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Concepts/overview.htm "The Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Email Delivery service provides a fast and reliable managed solution for sending secured, high-volume marketing and transactional emails.")

[Video: Overview](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-hWI_wlojKA)

[First steps with Email Delivery](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/gettingstarted.htm "Configure Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Email Delivery to use the functionalities of the Email Delivery service. You can configure using the Console, REST API, SDKs, CLI, or Terraform.")

[Best practices for email deliverability](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/deliverabilitybestpractices.htm "Learn about the best practices to deliver bulk, marketing, or transactional emails to opt-in recipients.")

[Email Delivery service limits](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/General/service-limits/default.htm#Email_Delivery_Limits)

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/networking/email-delivery/)

#### Reporting

[Log details for Email Delivery](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Logging/Reference/details_for_emaildelivery.htm)

[Email Delivery metrics](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/metricsalarms.htm "Monitor the health, capacity, and performance of the Email Delivery service by using metrics, alarms, and notifications.")

[Manage the suppression list](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Tasks/managingsuppressionlist.htm "As you begin to send email, Email Delivery automatically adds email addresses with bounce codes showing permanent failures, or user complaints to the suppression list to protect the sender reputation. Email Delivery doesn't send any messages to these recipients in the future.")

#### FAQs

[Click here for frequently asked questions](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/networking/email-delivery/faq/)

[List-Unsubscribe](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/list-unsubscribe.htm "List-Unsubscribe is an industry-standard email feature that provides recipients with a simplified way to opt-out of receiving further emails from a sender, using their email client. It uses special email headers to provide participating email clients with a method to send unsubscribe requests. It is a best practice for senders to include these headers in the email to decrease spam complaint rates, and increase overall engagement.")

#### Set Up Email Delivery

[Creating a Group and User](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/gettingstarted_topic-create-group.htm "Learn how to create a group and user, and add a user to a group.")

[Creating User Permissions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/gettingstarted_topic-create-policy.htm "Learn how to create a policy to allow a group to manage email-family resources.")

[Creating SMTP Credentials](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/gettingstarted_topic-create-smtp-credentials.htm "Learn how to create SMTP credentials.")

[Creating an Email Domain](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/gettingstarted_topic-create-email-domain.htm "Learn how to create a DNS email domain to send bulk emails from approved senders.")

[Creating an Approved Sender](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/gettingstarted_topic-Create_an_approved_sender.htm "Learn how to create an approved sender who can send emails using the OCI Email Delivery service.")

[Setting up Email Submissions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/gettingstarted_topic-set-up-email-submissions.htm "Use OCI Email Delivery for a fast and reliable managed solution to submit secure, high-volume marketing, and transactional emails using the Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure (HTTPS) interface or the Simple Mail Transfer Protocol (SMTP) interface.")

[Sending an Email](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/gettingstarted_topic-Begin_sending_email.htm "Learn to use the Email Delivery service by configuring Oracle Cloud and third-party applications to send an email.")

[Validating Email Delivery](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/gettingstarted_topic-Validate_email.htm "Learn how to use the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Logging service to validate whether an email is delivered.")

#### Developer Tools

[API for Email Delivery](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/emaildelivery/latest/)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

[Quick start in Git hub](https://github.com/oracle-quickstart/oci-ed-setup)

#### Community

[Email-related blog posts](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/authors/Blog-Author/CORE94AB2886640E403B93A7D9469F1C5AD9/josh-nason)

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

#### Troubleshooting

[Troubleshoot Email Delivery](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Concepts/troubleshooting.htm "Use troubleshooting information to identify and address common issues that can occur while working Email Delivery.")

[Troubleshoot Email Delivery with service logs](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/post/announcing-availability-of-oracle-cloud-infrastructure-oci-email-delivery-service-logs)

#### Integrations

[Oracle Application Express](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/apex.htm "Use Oracle APEX to send emails through the Email Delivery service.")

[Postfix](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/postfix.htm "Use Postfix to send emails through the Email Delivery service.")

[JavaMail](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/javamail.htm "Use JavaMail to send emails through the Email Delivery service.")

[PeopleSoft](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Email/Reference/peoplesoft.htm "Use PeopleSoft to send emails through the Email Delivery service.")

#### Support

[Get help and contact support](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

# Marketplace

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Marketplace/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Marketplace
===========

Oracle Cloud Marketplace is an online store that offers a catalog of listings from approved and registered publishers. Customers (consumers) use Marketplace to find an image, stack, container image, helm chart, and more, and deploy it on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI). Partners (publishers) use Marketplace to list and manage products and services for OCI customers.

[What's New](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/marketplace/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about Marketplace](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Marketplace/overview-marketplace.htm)

[Commercial guide](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Marketplace/commercial-guide.htm)

[Policies](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Marketplace/marketplace-policies.htm)

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/marketplace)

#### Developer Tools

[API for Marketplace](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/marketplace/)

[CLI for Marketplace](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/marketplace.html)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

#### Customers-Consumers

[Use Marketplace in the Console](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Marketplace/introduction.htm)

[Use Marketplace on the web](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Marketplace/cloud-marketplace.htm)

[Service Catalog](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Marketplace/overview_of_service_catalog.htm)

#### FAQs

[Marketplace FAQ](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Marketplace/marketplace-faqs.htm)

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

Analytics Cloud
===============

Analytics empowers business analysts and consumers with modern, AI-powered, self-service analytics capabilities for data preparation, visualization, enterprise reporting, augmented analysis, and natural language processing.

Fusion Data Intelligence
========================

Oracle Fusion Data Intelligence provides analytics for Oracle Applications Cloud, powered by Autonomous Data Warehouse and Oracle Analytics.

Oracle Blockchain Platform
==========================

Get Started
-----------

Create and manage your blockchain network with Oracle Blockchain Platform on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure

### Learn

### Get Started

*   [Create an instance](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/blockchain-cloud&id=BCOSA-GUID-700E2CC4-EB07-4F9D-BD4F-67824176CA3F)

# Functions

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Functions
=========

Functions is a serverless platform that enables you to create, run, and scale business logic without managing any infrastructure.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/functions/)

#### Get Started

[QuickStart on Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsquickstartcloudshell.htm "Find out how to get set up and running quickly on Cloud Shell using this OCI Functions QuickStart.")

[QuickStart on local host](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsquickstartlocalhost.htm "Find out how to get set up and running quickly on a local host using this OCI Functions QuickStart.")

[Learn about Functions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Concepts/functionsoverview.htm "Learn how the Functions service lets you create, run, and scale business logic without managing any infrastructure .")

[Review key concepts](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Concepts/functionsconcepts.htm "Find out about the key concepts you need to understand before using OCI Functions.")

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/cloud-native/functions/)

#### Create Functions

[Create applications](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionscreatingapps.htm "Find out how to create applications with OCI Functions.")

[Create functions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsuploading.htm "Find out how to create and deploy functions to OCI Functions using Fn Project CLI commands.")

[Use existing Docker images](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionscreatingfunctions.htm "Find out how to create functions from existing Docker images with OCI Functions.")

[Use custom Dockerfiles](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsusingcustomdockerfiles.htm "Find out how to use your own custom Dockerfile with OCI Functions.")

#### Manage Functions

[List functions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsviewingfunctionsapps.htm "Find out how to list functions with OCI Functions.")

[Update functions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsupdatingfunctions.htm "Find out how to update existing functions in the OCI Functions server.")

[Scan function images](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsimagescanning.htm "Find out how to enable and disable scans of function images pushed to Container Registry using OCI Functions, and how to check scan results for vulnerabilities found in those function images.")

[Sign function images](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsenforcingsignedimagesfromocir.htm "Find out how to sign function images, and how to enforce the use of signed images from Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Registry when deploying when deploying and invoking functions using OCI Functions.")

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://forums.oracle.com/ords/apexds/domain/dev-community/category/15686-platform?tags=cloud-applications,cloud-infrastructure)

#### Sample Functions

[Sample functions on GitHub](https://github.com/oracle/oracle-functions-samples)

[Follow solution playbooks](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsdownloadingsamples_topic-Solution_Playbooks.htm "Find out about the solution playbooks that are available to help you learn how to use OCI Functions.")

[Learn from reference architectures](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsdownloadingsamples_topic-Reference_Architectures.htm "Find out about the reference architectures that are available to help you learn how to use OCI Functions.")

[Try Developer tutorials](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/developer/home.htm#home__functions)

#### Invoke Functions

[Invoke functions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsinvokingfunctions.htm "Find out the different ways to invoke functions deployed to OCI Functions.")

[Invoke functions from other services](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsintegratingwithother.htm "Find out how to invoke functions in OCI Functions from other Oracle Cloud Infrastructure services.")

#### Integrate Functions

[Integrate with other products](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsintegratingwithotheroracleproducts.htm "Find out how to integrate functions in OCI Functions with other Oracle products.")

[Access file systems](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsaccessinglocalfilesystem.htm "Find out how to access file systems from running functions deployed to OCI Functions.")

[Pass parameters](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionspassingconfigparams-about.htm "Find out about passing custom configuration parameters to running functions with OCI Functions.")

#### Developer Tools

[Functions API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/functions/)

[OCI CLI for Functions](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/fn.html)

[Terraform Registry](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/oracle/oci/latest/docs)

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

#### Observe Functions

[Function logs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsexportingfunctionlogfiles.htm "Find out how to store and view function logs with OCI Functions.")

[Function tracing](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionstracing.htm "Find out how to enable tracing and view function traces when debugging with OCI Functions.")

[Function metrics](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Reference/functionsmetrics.htm "Find out about the metrics emitted by OCI Functions in the oci_faas metric namespace.")

[Troubleshooting](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionstroubleshooting.htm "Find out how to troubleshoot problems with OCI Functions, and possible solutions to common issues.")

#### Control access

[Control access to functions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsrestrictinguseraccess.htm "Find out how to control the functions that users can invoke and manage in OCI Functions.")

[Container permissions](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsrunningasunprivileged.htm "Find out about the permissions granted to containers running functions with OCI Functions.")

[Private network access](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Concepts/functionsprivateaccess.htm "Find out about how OCI Functions supports private network access to enable private communication between functions and other resources.")

#### Functions and Fn Project

[Upgrade Fn Project CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionsupgradingfncli.htm "Find out how to upgrade the Fn Project CLI for use with OCI Functions.")

[Manage FDKs and languages](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Tasks/functionssupportedlanguageversions.htm "Find out about the Function Development Kits (FDKs) used by OCI Functions to support different languages.")

[Differences between Functions and Fn Project](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Functions/Concepts/functionsdiffswithfn.htm "Find out about the differences between OCI Functions and Fn Project.")

#### Support

[Support Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

Oracle WebLogic Server for OKE
==============================

Quickly provision Oracle WebLogic Server domains and supporting resources in Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE) using Resource Manager and Jenkins CI/CD pipeline jobs.

Get Started
-----------

### Start Here

*   [Learn about the Components](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-container&id=WLKUG-GUID-D2C79FB8-EC82-41B2-9D39-5FC9D71D1242)

*   [Oracle Cloud Cost Estimator](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/cost-estimator.html)
    

### Create

# Managed Cloud Self Service Platform

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Managed Cloud Self Service Platform
===================================

Oracle Managed Cloud Self-Service Platform provides a range of tools and services designed to manage CEMLIs (Configurations, Extensions, Modifications, Localizations, and Integrations) associated with your Oracle E-Business Suite environment.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/managed-cloud-self-service/)

#### Get Started

[About Oracle Managed Cloud Self-Service Platform](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_about_oracle_managed_cloud_self_service_platform.htm#top "The Oracle Managed Cloud Self-Service Platform initiative enables a modernized experience by offering self-service capabilities to MCS customers at OCI. The Self-Service Platform delivers capabilities and automation used by MCS Operations directly for use by the customer. It improves service agility while delivering a modernized managed application lifecycle experience.")

[Requirements](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_requirements.htm#top "Learn about the minimum requirements to use Oracle Managed Cloud Self-Service Platform.")

[Working with Instances](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_working_with_instances.htm#top "Learn how to use the Instances tab to add new instances, edit existing instances, and select an instance to associate your CEMLIs with.")

[Using Packager](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_using_packager.htm#top "Learn how to use the Packager option, the core tool in Oracle Automated CEMLI Execution (ACE).")

[Setting Preferences](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_setting_preferences.htm#top "Learn how to set preferences.")

#### Key Features

[Using Reports](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_using_reports.htm#top "Learn how to use the Reports tab to search CEMLI data and export such data to Excel.")

[Using Configurations](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_using_configurations.htm#top "Learn how to use the configurations.")

[Using Bounce](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_using_bounce.htm#top "Learn how the OCI users can work with self-service Bounce to shut down, start up, and bounce DB Tier and MidTiers and/or MidTier components (individual or combination of components).")

[CEMLI File Object Types](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_cemli_file_object_types.htm#top "Learn about all the CEMLI file objects that are supported in Oracle Automated CEMLI Execution Release 18.1.")

[CEMLI and Data Fix File Header Format](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_cemli_file_header_format.htm#top "Learn the correct format for CEMLI file headers.")

#### **Working with Oracle Managed Cloud Self-Service Platform**

[Using CEMLI](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_using_cemli.htm#top "Learn about the actions that you can take on CEMLI files using Oracle Managed Cloud Self-Service Platform.")

[Building Patches](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_building_patches.htm#top "You can now use the files you have uploaded to create custom patches which can be applied to your environments.")

[Scheduling Custom Patches for Deployment (Execution)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_scheduling_custom_patches_for_deployment_execution.htm#top "Learn how to schedule custom patches for deployment.")

[Using Data Fix](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_using_datafix.htm#top "Learn about the actions you can take on Data fix files using Oracle Managed Cloud Self-Service Platform.")

[Scheduling Data Fixes for Deployment (Execution)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_scheduling_datafixes_for_deployment_execution.htm#top "Learn how to schedule data fixes for deployment.")

#### Licensing

[Licensing Information](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/managed-cloud-self-service/mgdcloudss_licensing_information.htm#top "Learn about the licensing information on third-party products included with Oracle Managed Cloud Self-Service Platform.")

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://community.oracle.com/tech/apps-infra/categories/18430-cloud-infrastructure)

#### Developer Tools

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

#### Support

[Support Requests](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/contactingsupport.htm)

[Create a service request](https://support.oracle.com/)

All Pages

Events
======

The Events service helps you create automation based on the state changes of resources throughout your tenancy.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/events/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about Events](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Events/Concepts/eventsoverview.htm "Create automation based on the state changes of resources throughout your tenancy.")

[Set up for Events](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Events/Concepts/eventsgetstarted.htm#Setup)

[Tutorial: Create a rule (Console)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Events/Concepts/eventsgetstarted.htm#Console)

[Tutorial: Create a rule (CLI)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Events/Concepts/eventsgetstarted.htm#CLI)

[Services that produce events](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Events/Reference/eventsproducers.htm "Review the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure services that emit events.")

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/events-service/)

#### Match Events to Filters

[Learn how filtering works](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Events/Concepts/filterevents.htm#Background)

[Use filters to route events](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Events/Concepts/filterevents.htm#matching)

#### Manage Rules for Events

[Prerequisites for rules](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Events/Task/managingrules.htm#prereq)

[Workflow for setting up rules](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Events/Task/managingrules.htm#Working)

#### Developer Tools

[SDKs and the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm)

[Cloud Shell](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/devcloudshellintro.htm)

[API for Events](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/events/)

[CLI for Events](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/cmdref/events.html)

#### Events Messages and Metrics

[Contents of an event message](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Events/Reference/eventenvelopereference.htm "Learn about the contents of an event message.")

[Event metrics](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/Events/Reference/eventsmetrics.htm "Understand the metrics emitted by the metric namespace oci_cloudevents (the Events service).")

#### Community

[Oracle Cloud Infrastructure blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/cloud-infrastructure/)

[Cloud infrastructure community forum](https://forums.oracle.com/ords/apexds/domain/dev-community/category/15686-platform?tags=cloud-applications,cloud-infrastructure)

Oracle AI Data Platform Workbench
=================================

Oracle AI Data Platform Workbench simplifies cataloging, ingesting, and analyzing data for data professionals in an organization. The Oracle AI Data Platform Workbench service provides the platform and the framework to create data analytics pipelines.

Get Started
-----------

### Before You Start

*   [Learn about Oracle AI Data Platform Workbench](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/ai-data-platform&id=GUID-611EB4E3-80FF-48DD-8158-D460EBBB7FBD)
    
*   [Review IAM Policies](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/ai-data-platform&id=GUID-C534FDF6-B678-4025-B65A-7217D9D9B3DA)
    

### Set Up and First Steps

*   [Create your first AI Data Platform Workbench](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/ai-data-platform&id=GUID-86E22B67-44AB-4315-977E-354BD4C100D5)
    
*   [Access your AI Data Platform Workbench](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/ai-data-platform&id=GUID-97C11774-6E36-401F-A82F-275A2E73540F)
    
*   [Next steps after setup](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/ai-data-platform&id=GUID-9DD16ED0-B8ED-4256-8231-8461CBC5208A)
    
*   [Manage what users can see and do](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/ai-data-platform&id=GUID-DCB725F3-0C06-45C2-B7A9-67731A6FC037)
    

### Manage Data

*   [Learn about Master Catalog](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/ai-data-platform&id=GUID-54889C02-6035-4D96-BAF9-67D1E4748BBC)
    
*   [Manage data with external catalogs](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/ai-data-platform&id=GUID-971F5561-FF4E-4E78-84A7-B46F5E44540A)
    
*   [Automate metadata extraction](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/ai-data-platform&id=GUID-9FE356D2-1C10-4504-8C2A-3712148C9991)
    
*   [Share your data](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/ai-data-platform&id=GUID-86C3D15D-83C0-4AE7-9A97-B6A7F7598D22)
    

### Connect to Data

*   [Ingest data from internal or external sources](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/ai-data-platform&id=GUID-A317B797-0928-4E19-8CF2-2C9FE0A719DE)
    
*   [Connect to Oracle Analytics Cloud](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/ai-data-platform&id=GUID-681444E7-3EB0-4002-B1E9-E09AF267A76F)
    

### Develop

*   [Develop code in notebooks](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/ai-data-platform&id=GUID-3F0138DE-749B-45BB-90F8-41368F0A166B)
    
*   [Learn about using Workflows](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/ai-data-platform&id=GUID-B6FEE8C8-80D4-4F27-9439-27AA140C7E36)
    
*   [Use parameters to modify jobs and tasks](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/ai-data-platform&id=GUID-AA30F608-810A-4FBC-903A-FED90A15EC55)
    
*   [SQL grammar for AI Data Platform Workbench objects](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/ai-data-platform&id=GUID-D067CE79-ED4E-4BB7-9D5D-C6E0BB979EB5)
    
*   [Use SDK and CLI to develop your AI Data Platform](https://github.com/oracle-samples/aidataplatform-sdk)
    

### Work with AI

*   [Build your first AI agent](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/ai-data-platform&id=GUID-E6A3EFD3-2976-403F-BB64-2B5B1AD65DB6)
    
*   [Connect your agents with MCP servers](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/ai-data-platform&id=GUID-F832D52C-89C6-4B2F-AA91-30C9411F4BFA)
    
*   [Run your first machine learning experiment](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/ai-data-platform&id=GUID-FD484342-FFDF-4587-AEE7-177AC7C65BCE)

# Welcome to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/GSG/Concepts/baremetalintro.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Welcome to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure
======================================

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) is a set of complementary cloud services that enable you to build and run a range of applications and services in a highly available hosted environment. OCI provides high-performance compute capabilities (as physical hardware instances) and storage capacity in a flexible overlay virtual network that is securely accessible from your on-premises network.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/)

#### Learn  
![learn](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/GSG/images/gsg_learn.png)

[Get to Know the Console](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Concepts/console.htm)

[Find developer tools and resources](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/devtoolshome.htm)

[Browse videos](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLKCk3OyNwIzuA-dqI5vkhZVIc_cxHCkp5)

#### Create  
![""](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/GSG/images/gsg-create.png)  

[Launch an instance](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Compute/tutorials/first-linux-instance/overview.htm)

[Build a load balancer](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/loadbalancing.htm)

[Put data into object storage](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/addingbuckets.htm)

[Get started with the CLI](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/GSG/Tasks/gettingstartedwiththeCLI.htm)

# Generative AI Agents

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai-agents/home.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Generative AI Agents
====================

Generative AI Agents is a fully managed service that combines the power of large language models (LLMs) with AI technologies to create intelligent virtual agents that can provide personalized, context-aware, and highly engaging customer experiences.

[What's new](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/releasenotes/services/generative-ai-agents/)

#### Get Started

[Learn about Generative AI Agents](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai-agents/overview.htm "OCI Generative AI Agents is a fully managed service that combines the power of large language models (LLMs) with AI technologies to create intelligent virtual agents that can provide personalized, context-aware, and highly engaging customer experiences.")

[Model selection](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai-agents/model-selection.htm "The OCI Generative AI Agents service supports Model Selection, where you can select a large language model (LLM) as the routing LLM for your agent during creation or editing. You can select from on-demand models hosted in the OCI Generative AI service or create and use dedicated AI cluster endpoints for supported models.")

[RAG tool guidelines](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai-agents/RAG-tool-object-storage-guidelines.htm "Review the following sections to prepare Object Storage data for RAG tools in Generative AI Agents.")

[SQL tool guidelines](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai-agents/sqltool-guidelines.htm "Set up a Structured Query Language (SQL) tool in an agent to generate SQL query statements from natural language queries, and optionally run the queries.")

[Product page](https://www.oracle.com/artificial-intelligence/generative-ai/agents/)

#### Chat with an agent

[Get access](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai-agents/iam-policies.htm "You can get access to Generative AI Agents resources with OCI Identity and Access Management (IAM) policies.")

[Create an agent](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai-agents/create-agent.htm "Create an agent with one or more tools, and set up an agent endpoint in the Generative AI Agents service.")

[Create a RAG tool](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai-agents/RAG-tool-create.htm "In Generative AI Agents, each agent must have one or more tools. You can create an agent's tool during or after creating the agent. For both options, creating a tool has the same steps. This section shows you the steps to create a RAG tool.")

[Create a SQL tool](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai-agents/sqltool-add.htm "In Generative AI Agents, each agent must have one or more tools. You can create an agent's tool during or after creating the agent. For both options, creating a tool has the same steps. This section shows you the steps to create a SQL tool.")

[Create a function calling tool](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai-agents/function-calling-tool-create.htm "In Generative AI Agents, each agent must have one or more tools. You can create an agent's tool during or after creating the agent. For both options, creating a tool has the same steps. This section shows you the steps to create a function calling tool.")

[Create an endpoint](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai-agents/create-endpoint.htm)

[Start chatting](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai-agents/chatting.htm "Chat with an agent that you have created in the Generative AI Agents service.")

#### Agent Development Kit (ADK)

[About ADK](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/generative-ai-agents/adk/api-reference/introduction.htm)

[Quickstart](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/generative-ai-agents/adk/api-reference/quickstart.htm)

[Examples](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/generative-ai-agents/adk/api-reference/examples.htm)

[Tutorials](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/generative-ai-agents/tutorials.htm "Generative AI Agents tutorials show you how to complete service-related tasks with step-by-step instructions.")

[Best practices](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/generative-ai-agents/adk/api-reference/best-practices.htm)

[GitHub Python (Source Code)](https://github.com/oracle/oci-python-sdk/tree/master/src/oci/addons/adk)

[GitHub Java (Source Code)](https://github.com/oracle/oci-java-sdk/tree/master/bmc-addons/bmc-adk)

#### Community

[AI at Oracle](https://www.oracle.com/artificial-intelligence/)

[Oracle AI and Data Science Blog](https://blogs.oracle.com/ai-and-datascience/)

#### Developer Tools

[Management API](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/generative-ai-agents/)

# Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure - Get Started

[Go to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud/index.html#)

 Search

![](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud/sp_common/shared-images/Java-cloud-270.png)

1.  [Home](https://docs.oracle.com/index.html)
    
2.  [Cloud](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/index.html)
    
3.  [Cloud Platform](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/index.html)
    
4.  [WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud/index.html)
    

Oracle WebLogic Server for Oracle Cloud Infrastructure
======================================================

The Oracle WebLogic Server complete implementation of the Java Platform, Enterprise Edition (Java EE) specification provides a standard set of APIs for creating distributed Java applications that can access a wide variety of services, such as databases, messaging services, and connections to external enterprise systems.

Oracle WebLogic Server for OCI Stack Oracle WebLogic Server for OCI Image

Oracle WebLogic Server for Stack
--------------------------------

Quickly provision an Oracle WebLogic Server domain in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) for developing and deploying Java Enterprise Edition (Java EE) applications.

### Start Here

*   [Learn about Oracle WebLogic Server for OCI](https://www.oracle.com/java/weblogic/weblogic-for-oracle-cloud-infrastructure/index.html)
    
*   [Learn about the Product Components](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud&id=WLCUG-GUID-D5F1469F-C9C5-4A7C-99B6-8DA7F1C16F79)
    
*   [Create your first Domain](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud&id=WLCGS)
    
    Related
    
    *   [Video](https://apexapps.oracle.com/pls/apex/f?p=44785:265:0::::P265_CONTENT_ID:28465)
        
    
*   [Oracle Cloud Cost Estimator](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/cost-estimator.html)
    

### Create

### Manage

*   [Access the WebLogic Console](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud&id=WLCUG-GUID-59CBD7D3-061F-4F9D-89EE-E8591D8D3F4F)
    
*   [Start and Stop a Domain](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud&id=WLCUG-GUID-5DD381DA-CB02-48C0-A3C0-579673991FBD)

*   [Scale a Domain](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud&id=WLCUG-GUID-427B73BA-4AEA-49B2-BB73-AD46B3CBF2E9)

Oracle WebLogic Server for Image
--------------------------------

Create Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Compute instances with the entitlement to create Oracle WebLogic Server domains. Quickly install Oracle WebLogic Server using the WebLogic Server and Java Development Toolkit binaries provided on disk.

### Start Here

*   [About Images](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud&id=WLCPI-GUID-706903FB-08F6-4143-B444-0AC343FF551D)

*   [Use an Image](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud&id=WLCPI-GUID-EC3BE540-01F6-44E1-9908-AC08B1CD36F6)
    

### Create

*   [Compartment Policies](https://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=en/cloud/paas/weblogic-cloud&id=WLCPI-GUID-5F2A547A-5B90-4F78-B3B9-3D8185756491)

# Visual Builder

Visual Builder
==============

Oracle Visual Builder is a cloud-based software development platform and a hosted environment for your application development infrastructure. It provides an open source standards-based solution to develop, collaborate on, and deploy applications within Oracle Cloud.

Getting Started
---------------

*   [About Oracle Visual Builder](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/visual-builder/doc/overview-oracle-visual-builder-generation-2.html#VBOCI-GUID-34469499-09C7-4819-9A06-E22E93BA4082)
    
*   [Availability](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/visual-builder/doc/overview-oracle-visual-builder-generation-2.html#VBOCI-GUID-A4615B8F-6356-458E-BFF9-806E03D573EA)
    
*   [Create the Visual Builder Instance](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/visual-builder/doc/create-and-set-your-visual-builder-generation-2-instance.html#VBOCI-GUID-E8C04103-B20C-437D-BE00-C99EDC45A8FD)
    
*   [Set Up Users and Groups](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/visual-builder/doc/setting-users-and-groups-oci-gen-2-1.html#GUID-EFC3AE73-99CC-4949-B452-B6177BF3517E)
    
*   [View and Manage the Instance](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/visual-builder/doc/manage-visual-builder-instance.html#VBOCI-GUID-9B8F063B-1439-44CE-B858-96C4CC2194A6)
