# Service Change Announcements

### [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/home.htm)

* * *

All Pages

[Skip to main content](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#dcoc-content-body)

Service Change Announcements
============================

Review details about Oracle Cloud Infrastructure breaking changes, such as deprecated features, deprecated APIs, and service behavior changes.

API Gateway
-----------

[Deprecated Ciphers](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Oracle Cloud Infrastructure API Gateway service support for certain legacy ciphers is deprecated.

**Date Announced:** March, 2024

**Date in Effect:** April 1, 2025

**Details:** Effective April 1, 2025, the API Gateway service no longer supports the following legacy ciphers:

*   ECDHE-RSA-AES128-SHA256
*   ECDHE-RSA-AES256-SHA384
*   DHE-RSA-AES256-SHA256
*   DHE-RSA-AES128-SHA256

**Does this impact me?** After April 1, 2025, an API gateway will no longer include the legacy ciphers in the list of supported ciphers when establishing a connection with an API client or with a back-end service. An API client or a back-end service that only supports legacy ciphers will no longer be able to connect to an API gateway.

**What do I need to do?** Make sure that API clients and back-end services connecting to API gateways support one or more of the ciphers that continue to be supported by the API Gateway service (see [Supported TLS Versions and Ciphers](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewaysupportedtlsversionsciphers.htm)
).

[X-XSS-Protection response header deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Oracle Cloud Infrastructure API Gateway service support for the X-XSS-Protection response header is deprecated.

**Date Announced:** 11 February, 2026

**Date in Effect:** 11 February, 2027

**Details:** Effective 11 February 2027, the API Gateway service no longer returns a default value for the X-XSS-Protection header on HTTP responses.

**Does this impact me?** The X-XSS-Protection header is non-standard and is no longer supported by modern browsers (although it might still be used by legacy browsers).

**What do I need to do?** If you serve web applications via an API gateway, make sure that an appropriate Content-Security-Policy header is in place, either served from the backend or generated using a header transformation policy. You can also use a header transformation policy if you need to maintain the behavior of the X-XSS-Protection header. For more information about header transformation policies, see [Transforming Incoming Requests and Outgoing Responses](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/APIGateway/Tasks/apigatewaymodifyingresponsesrequests.htm)
.

Application Dependency Management
---------------------------------

[Deprecated fields in Vulnerability Audit API](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Some fields in the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure's [Application Dependencies Vulnerability](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/adm/20220421/VulnerabilityAudit/ListApplicationDependencyVulnerabilities)
 API is deprecated.

**Date Announced:** February 10, 2026

**Date in Effect:** February 10, 2026

**Details:** Following fields are deprecated from the **ListApplicationDependencyVulnerabilities** API:

*   `cvssV2Score`
*   `cvssV3Score`
*   `isFoundInKnowledgeBase`

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you're using the deprecated fields (`cvssv2score` and `cvssv3score`) in the ListApplicationDependencyVulnerabilities API.

**What do I need to do?** Use the new **ListVulnerabilities** API to get the information related to `cvssV2Score` and `cvssV3Score` that's available in the metrics of each vulnerability. No replacement is provided for `isFoundInKnowledgeBase` field.

Autonomous Recovery Service
---------------------------

[Deprecated subnetId Attribute](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The required `subnetId` attribute from the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure's [CreateRecoveryServiceSubnet](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/recovery-service/latest/RecoveryServiceSubnet/CreateRecoveryServiceSubnet)
 API is deprecated.

The [CreateRecoveryServiceSubnet](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/recovery-service/latest/RecoveryServiceSubnet/CreateRecoveryServiceSubnet)
 API can instead use the optional `Subnets` attribute, which later changes to become a required attribute.

**Date Announced:** May 2023.

**Date in Effect:** May 2024.

**Details:** Prior to this service change, the `subnetId` attribute can be used to specify only a single subnet to be associated with a Recovery Service subnet. After this service change, the `subnetId` attribute is ignored and `subnets` will be required attribute. The `subnets` attribute can be used to specify multiple subnets to be associated with a Recovery Service subnet.

**Does this impact me?** If you have custom scripts or Terraform scripts that are referencing the [CreateRecoveryServiceSubnet](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/recovery-service/latest/RecoveryServiceSubnet/CreateRecoveryServiceSubnet)
 API and are explicitly using the `subnetId` attribute, you might want to modify the scripts to remove this attribute and use `subnets` instead.

**What do I need to do?** When using [OCI SDKs](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm#Software_Development_Kits_and_Command_Line_Interface)
 and [command line tools](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/)
, you must choose to update your custom scripts to use the `subnets` attribute. After May 2024, the `subnets` attribute is a required field.

Big Data Service
----------------

[Oracle Big Data Service with Cloudera Distribution of Hadoop – BDS CDH expansion restriction](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** On January 31, 2023, the agreement between Oracle and Cloudera expires. As a result, starting on January 31, Oracle Big Data Service will no longer support using the Cloudera Distribution including Apache Hadoop (CDH) to launch new clusters or add nodes or cores to existing clusters beyond a set limit. This change doesn't impact ongoing support of existing Big Data Service CDH clusters.

This change is applicable only to Big Data Service CDH. This change does _not_ impact customers who use on-premises Oracle Big Data Appliance (BDA) or Big Data Service with Oracle Distribution of Hadoop (ODH).

**Date Announced:** December 13, 2022

**Date in Effect:** January 31, 2023

**Details:** On January 31, 2023, the limit for Big Data Service CDH expansion of the number of compute cores (OCPUs) freezes. The number of compute cores in a customer's tenancy on January 31 becomes the maximum number of cores allowed for that tenancy, and no further expansion will be allowed.

**Does this impact me?** This change is only applicable to Big Data Service with CDH. Big Data Service with Oracle Distribution of Hadoop (ODH) is not impacted in any way.

For example, on January 31, 2023 if a tenancy has 2 BDS CDH clusters with 15 nodes each with a total of 120 OCPUs, then after January 31, 2023 the maximum limit for OCPUs will be set to 120 and cannot be increased beyond 120 after that date. For support issues such as replacing failed nodes, new nodes may be added up to that limit. Users can also decrease the number of cores and increase back to the same limit (for example, 120).

**Next steps:** Oracle recommends planning and implementing any required Big Data Service CDH cluster expansions before January 31, 2023. For customers who want to avoid this expansion constraint after that date, we recommend migrating to Big Data Service ODH.

**About Big Data Service with ODH:** In 2022, Oracle released [Big Data Service ODH](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/bigdata/home.htm)
, which we developed as a cloud native service for our big data customers. Oracle provides continuous investment and support for ODH, with no licensing requirements from Cloudera. ODH has key enterprise-focused features, including autoscaling, Kerberos, Active Directory integration, HDFS connector for Object Storage, and Bootstrap scripts. And it's one of the most cost-efficient big data products in the market.

Big Data Service ODH has [multiple versions](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/bigdata/update-overview.htm)
 with the latest and previous versions of Hadoop components for compatibility with newer and older application stacks. It's also verified with [WANdisco Data Migrator](https://wandisco.com/partners/oracle)
 for easier migrations at scale and uses Oracle Cloud Lift services to work with Oracle engineers for migrations. For information about migrating to Big Data Service ODH, see the [documentation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/bigdata/migrate-overview.htm)
.

Billing
-------

[Deprecated Usage Reports](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Usage reports on the **Cost and Usage Reports** Console page are deprecated.

**Date Announced:** October 10, 2024

**Date in Effect:** January 31, 2025

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you have usage reports in your tenancy.

**What do I need to do?** Use [cost reports](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Billing/Concepts/costusagereportsoverview.htm)
 in the OCI proprietary and FOCUS formats to analyze your consumption. You can continue to access your existing usage report CSV files until July 31, 2025.

Block Volume
------------

[Full backups removed from Oracle defined backup policies](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Oracle defined backup policies no longer include full volume backups.

**Date Announced:** November 3, 2020

**Date in Effect:** November 3, 2021

**Details:** Starting November 3, 2021, Oracle defined backup policies no longer include full volume backups. All volume backups generated from Oracle defined policies are now incremental backups. This change applies to existing volume backup policy assignments and new volume backup policy assignments. This change does not impact user defined policies, which remain unchanged.

**Does this impact me?** If you have assigned an Oracle defined policy to boot volumes or block volumes for scheduled backups, full backups are no longer generated after November 3, 2021.

**What do I need to do?** Incremental backups are functionally the same as full backups for data recovery purposes, for more information, see [Volume Backup types](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Block/Concepts/blockvolumebackups.htm#backuptype)
. No action is needed for data recovery scenarios. Some compliance scenarios may require scheduled full backups. For these compliance scenarios, change the backup policy assignment to a user defined backup policy prior to November 1, 2021. You can create a new user defined policy from an existing backup policy, see [Duplicating Existing Backup Policies](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Block/Tasks/schedulingvolumebackups.htm#duplicatepolicy)
.

Classic Migration
-----------------

[Classic Migration Service End of Life](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** End-of-life for OCI Classic Migration Service effective March 31, 2024.

**Date Announced:** March 04, 2024

**Date in Effect:** March 31, 2024

**Details:** Oracle announces the end-of-distribution and end-of-life updates for OCI Classic Migration Service effective March 31, 2024.

Classic Migration Service (formerly known as Application Migration Service) simplifies the migration of applications from Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Classic to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure. Classic Migration Service migrates applications such as Oracle Java Cloud Service, SOA Cloud Service, and Integration Classic applications, from Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Classic and Oracle Cloud@Customer to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure.

**Does this impact me?** There should be no impact to existing customers. Application versions that were supported by Classic Migrations have been obsolete for a number of years and customers are already running on more recent versions.

**What do I need to do?** No expected actions from customers. If you do find that you have a Classic application that needs to be migrated out then please contact the associated Cloud Application service.

Compute
-------

[PCI-NIC fault class for Compute bare metal instance health monitoring is deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The `PCI-NIC` fault class for Compute bare metal instance health monitoring is deprecated.

**Date Announced:** June 21, 2022

**Date in Effect:** June 21, 2023

**Details:** The `PCI-NIC` fault class provides information about a hardware issue with your bare metal instances, specifically when a fault in the instance network interface card (NIC) has been detected. The `PCI-NIC` fault class will no longer be emitted.

**Does this impact me?** When support ends, you will no longer receive [health monitoring notifications](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Compute/References/computehealthmonitoringguidance.htm)
 for the `PCI-NIC` fault class. [Infrastructure health metrics](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Compute/References/infrastructurehealthmetrics.htm)
 will no longer be emitted for the `PCI-NIC` fault class.

**What do I need to do?** Migrate to the `PCI` fault class for similar functionality. For more information, see [Compute Health Monitoring for Bare Metal Instances](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Compute/References/computehealthmonitoringguidance.htm)
 and [Infrastructure Health Metrics](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Compute/References/infrastructurehealthmetrics.htm)
.

[Compute instance management audit events migrating to version 2](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Compute instance management is deprecating audit events version 1 for instance pools and cluster networks. Customers need to migrate to audit events version 2.

**Date Announced:** June 27, 2026

**Date in Effect:** June 30, 2027

**Details:** Compute instance management is deprecating audit events version 1 for instance pools and cluster networks. Customers need to migrate to audit events version 2 by June 30, 2027. At that time, version 1 events will no longer be emitted.

To migrate to the new version of audit events, add the suffix V2 to an event name, for example "AttachLoadBalancerV2". Existing version 1 audit events will be emitted until 30 Jun 2027.

**Does this impact me?** Any scripts or code using APIs may be negatively impacted when the deprecation date is reached.

**What do I need to do?** Update any scripts or code to use the new naming convention. Update any data attributes as described in the following section.

#### Affected Events

The affected events and relevant data attribute changes are described below.

1.  **AttachInstancePoolInstance**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` contains the instance details that got attached compared to `data.stateChange.current.Current` in the previous version.
    
2.  **AttachLoadBalancer**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` contains instance pool details and `data.stateChange.previous` contains instance pool details before attachment. The previous version had pool details after attachment as part of `data.stateChange.current.Current`.
    
3.  **ChangeClusterNetworkCompartment**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` is null and `data.stateChange.previous` contains the cluster network details before it is moved to the destination compartment.
    
4.  **ChangeInstanceConfigurationCompartment**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` is null and `data.stateChange.previous` contains the instance configuration details before it is moved to the destination compartment.
    
5.  **ChangeInstancePoolCompartment**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` is null and `data.stateChange.previous` contains the instance pool details before it is moved to the destination compartment.
    
6.  **CreateClusterNetwork**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` contains the cluster network details that were created. In the previous version, `data.stateChange.current.Current` contained the cluster network details. `definedTags` and `freeformTags` can be referenced from `data.stateChange.current`.
    
7.  **CreateInstanceConfiguration**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` contains the instance configuration details that were created. In the previous version, `data.stateChange.current.Current` contained the instance configuration details. `definedTags` and `freeformTags` can be referenced from `data.stateChange.current`.
    
8.  **CreateInstancePool**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` contains the instance pool details that were created. In the previous version, `data.stateChange.current.Current` contained the instance pool details. `definedTags` and `freeformTags` can be referenced from `data.stateChange.current`.
    
9.  **DeleteInstanceConfiguration**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` is null and `data.stateChange.previous` contains instance configuration details before deletion.
    
10.  **DetachInstancePoolInstance**
    
    No change.
    
11.  **DetachLoadBalancer**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` contains the instance pool details after the detach call is invoked. In the previous version, `data.stateChange.current.Current` contained the instance pool details.
    
12.  **GetClusterNetwork**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` and `data.stateChange.previous` are both null, whereas in the previous version `data.stateChange.current` contained the cluster network details.
    
13.  **GetInstanceConfiguration**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` and `data.stateChange.previous` are both null, whereas in the previous version `data.stateChange.current` contained the instance configuration details.
    
14.  **GetInstancePool**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` and `data.stateChange.previous` are both null, whereas in the previous version `data.stateChange.current` contained the instance pool details.
    
15.  **GetInstancePoolInstance**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` and `data.stateChange.previous` are both null, whereas in the previous version `data.stateChange.current` contained the instance details.
    
16.  **GetInstancePoolLoadBalancerAttachment**
    
    Event type changed from `GetLoadBalancerAttachment` to `GetInstancePoolLoadBalancerAttachmentV2`.
    
17.  **LaunchInstanceConfiguration**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` refers to data of the instance being launched. In the previous version, it referred to data of the instance configuration being launched.
    
18.  **ListClusterNetworkInstances**
    
    `availabilityDomain` can now be fetched from `additionalDetails`. `limit` in `request.parameters` is only added if supplied by the customer. In the previous version, a default value of 100 was added if not supplied.
    
19.  **ListClusterNetworks**
    
    `limit` in `request.parameters` is only added if supplied by the customer. In the previous version, a default value of 100 was added if not supplied.
    
20.  **ListInstanceConfigurations**
    
    `limit` in `request.parameters` is only added if supplied by the customer. In the previous version, a default value of 100 was added if not supplied.
    
21.  **ListInstancePoolInstances**
    
    `availabilityDomain` can now be fetched from `additionalDetails`. `limit` in `request.parameters` is only added if supplied by the customer. In the previous version, a default value of 100 was added if not supplied.
    
22.  **ListInstancePools**
    
    `limit` in `request.parameters` is only added if supplied by the customer. In the previous version, a default value of 100 was added if not supplied.
    
23.  **ResetInstancePool**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` contains instance pool details, whereas in the previous version `data.stateChange.current.Current` contained them.
    
24.  **SoftresetInstancePool**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` contains instance pool details, whereas in the previous version `data.stateChange.current.Current` contained them.
    
25.  **SoftstopInstancePool**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` contains instance pool details, whereas in the previous version `data.stateChange.current.Current` contained them.
    
26.  **StartInstancePool**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` contains instance pool details, whereas in the previous version `data.stateChange.current.Current` contained them.
    
27.  **StopInstancePool**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` contains instance pool details, whereas in the previous version `data.stateChange.current.Current` contained them.
    
28.  **TerminateClusterNetwork**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` is null and `data.stateChange.previous` contains cluster network details before termination. In the previous version, `data.stateChange.current.Current` was populated after modification and `data.stateChange.previous` was null.
    
29.  **TerminateInstancePool**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` is null and `data.stateChange.previous` contains instance pool details before termination. In the previous version, `data.stateChange.current.Current` was populated after modification and `data.stateChange.previous` was null.
    
30.  **TerminationProceedInstancePoolInstance**
    
    No change.
    
31.  **UpdateClusterNetwork**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` and `data.stateChange.previous` contain cluster network details after and before modification respectively. In the previous version, these were at `data.stateChange.current.Current` and `data.stateChange.previous.Previous`. Current and previous sizes of the instance pool should be fetched from `data.stateChange.current` and `data.stateChange.previous` respectively. In the previous version, these were available as `instancePoolSize` and `previousInstancePoolSize` in `data.response.payload`.
    
32.  **UpdateInstanceConfiguration**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` and `data.stateChange.previous` contain instance configuration details after and before modification respectively. In the previous version, these were at `data.stateChange.current.Current` and `data.stateChange.previous.Previous`.
    
33.  **UpdateInstancePool**
    
    `data.stateChange.current` and `data.stateChange.previous` contain instance pool details after and before modification respectively. In the previous version, these were at `data.stateChange.current.Current` and `data.stateChange.previous.Previous`. Current and previous sizes of the instance pool should be fetched from `data.stateChange.current` and `data.stateChange.previous` respectively. In the previous version, these were available as `instancePoolSize` and `previousInstancePoolSize` in `data.response.payload`.
    

Connector Hub
-------------

[The lifecyleDetails property is deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The `lifecyleDetails` property at [ServiceConnector Reference](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/serviceconnectors/latest/ServiceConnector)
 is deprecated. Use the `lifecycleDetails` property instead.

**Date Announced:** March 25, 2025

**Date in Effect:** March 25, 2026

**Details:** The following property is deprecated:

`lifecyleDetails`

Use the following property instead:

`lifecycleDetails`.

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you have code that references the deprecated property, `lifecyleDetails`.

**What do I need to do?** Update your code to reference the new property, `lifecycleDetails`.

Data Integration Platform Cloud
-------------------------------

[Data Integration Platform Cloud End of Life](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Data Integration Platform Cloud End of Life

**Date Announced:** August 29, 2024

**Date in Effect:** December 12, 2024

**Details:** Effective December 12, 2024, Oracle Data Integration Platform Cloud (DIPC) will reach End of Life, and you will no longer be able to create new instances or receive support.

**Does this impact me?** Yes.

**What do I need to do?** Consider migrating as soon as possible to avoid any disruption to your DIPC processes. For guidance on migrating from DIPC, refer to the following links for the detailed migration steps:

*   [Migrating to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure GoldenGate](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/goldengate-service/migrating/index.html)
    
*   [Migrate Oracle Data Integrator](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/data-integration-platform-cloud/migrate/migrate-your-dipc-classic-instances-oci.html#DIPCM-GUID-B92DBE2B-5D3E-47C2-B494-51533EFED7E3)
    
*   [Migrate Enterprise Data Quality](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/data-integration-platform-cloud/migrate/migrate-your-dipc-classic-instances-oci.html#DIPCM-GUID-2AF2A8CD-C20A-46E3-926F-14582F04FAD4)
    

Data Labeling
-------------

[Data Labeling Service End of Life](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Data Labeling service is to be deprecated.

**Date Announced:** August 30, 2024

**Date in Effect:** August 30, 2025

**Details:** Effective August 30, 2025, the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Data Labeling service reaches end of life (EOL). Before the EOL date, we recommend that you migrate to using the open source labeling tools available in the [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Marketplace](https://cloudmarketplace.oracle.com/marketplace/oci)
 to label data.

**Does this impact me?** The Data Labeling service is no longer available for use after August 30, 2025. You can continue to use labels created before, but you must migrate immediately to avoid service disruption.

**What do I need to do?** You can use the open source labeling tools available in the [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Marketplace](https://cloudmarketplace.oracle.com/marketplace/oci)
 to create labels. We recommend that you use Open source alternatives for most of Data Labeling's functionality.

You can view the Data Labeling service only in regions where you have had activity within the past 6 months. To access the Data Labeling service in other regions, submit a request to the Data Labeling service queue.

For Vision custom models, the Data Labeling service is planned to be replaced by Label Studio, with no impact to existing models. For future custom models creation, label the data using Label Studio.

For Document Understanding, the document labeling functionality from the Data Labeling iservice s being integrated into it. Users can still access this feature in Document Understanding by navigating to the custom model training workflow.

Data Safe
---------

[Deprecated Data Safe APIs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Following APIs will be deprecated on September 22, 2025 when the target group feature goes GA in Data Safe. These APIs will be unavailable after one year of deprecation (September 22, 2026).

1.  `AuditPolicy`
2.  `AuditPolicyAnalyticsCollection`
3.  `AuditPolicyCollection`
4.  `DatabaseSecurityConfig`
5.  `DatabaseSecurityConfigCollection`

**Date Announced:** September 22, 2025

**Date in Effect:** September 22, 2026

**Details:** Starting September 22, 2025, `AuditPolicy`, `AuditPolicyCollection`, `AuditPolicyAnalyticsCollection`, `DatabaseSecurityConfig` and `DatabaseSecurityConfigCollection` APIs will be deprecated. From September 22, 2026 onwards, the deprecated APIs will be unavailable.

**Does this impact me?** This will impact all the API/SDK users.

**What do I need to do?** Update all the Data Safe client code to use the new APIs:

| Deprecated APIs | New API |
| --- | --- |
| `AuditPolicy` | `UnifiedAuditPolicy` |
| `AuditPolicyAnalyticsCollection` | `UnifiedAuditPolicyCollections` |
| `AuditPolicyCollection` | `UnifiedAuditPolicyCollections` |
| `DatabaseSecurityConfig` | `SecurityPolicyConfig` |
| `DatabaseSecurityConfigCollection` | `SecurityPolicyConfigCollection` |

Data Science
------------

[Deprecated IDCS Authentication Support for ML Applications](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Data Science is deprecating Oracle Identity Cloud Service (IDCS) authentication support for ML Applications.

**Date Announced:** March 10, 2026

**Date in Effect:** April 11, 2026

**Details:** Starting April 11, 2026, OCI Data Science is deprecating support for **IDCS** and **IDCS\_CUSTOM\_SERVICE** authentication methods in the ML application instance API to align with OCI security standards. This change ensures a more secure environment by removing outdated integration points and aligns the service with the OCI Identity architecture. Support would be removed across the Control Plane (CP) and Data Plane (DP).

**Does this impact me?** After the deprecation, deployments using IDCS based authentication isn't supported. New ML Application instances can't be created using IDCS as an Identity provider.

**What do I need to do?** All ML application instances must use Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Identity and Access Management (IAM) for authentication and authorization.

[Deprecated Support for TGI Service Managed Container](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Data Science AI Quick Actions is deprecating support for TGI service managed container.

**Date Announced:** December 08, 2025

**Date in Effect:** January 31, 2026

**Details:** Starting January 31, 2026, the existing service managed container Text Generation Inference (TGI) will be deprecated.

**Does this impact me?** After the deprecation, the deployment and updating of models using the TGI container through AI Quick Actions is no longer supported. Model deployments created with the TGI container will fail.

**What do I need to do?** Review the deprecation [guide](https://github.com/oracle-samples/oci-data-science-ai-samples/blob/main/ai-quick-actions/guide_to_upgrade_models_following_tgi_deprecation.md)
 to see if the service model you have deployed is in the list of models that are switching from the TGI to vLLM container and redeploy the model. If you deployed a custom model with the TGI container, you also need to redeploy the model.

Database
--------

### Autonomous AI Database Services

[Autonomous Data Warehouse Deprecated APIs (Feb. 15, 2019)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure's Autonomous AI Lakehouse APIs were deprecated on Feb. 15, 2019.

**Important!** Support for these Autonomous AI Lakehouse APIs will end on **Jul. 1, 2020**. Oracle recommends that you migrate your database workloads to the replacement APIs as soon as possible.

| Unsupported API | Replacement API |
| --- | --- |
| [CreateAutonomousDataWarehouse](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDataWarehouse/CreateAutonomousDataWarehouse) | [CreateAutonomousDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/CreateAutonomousDatabase) |
| [ListAutonomousDataWarehouses](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDataWarehouse/ListAutonomousDataWarehouses) | [ListAutonomousDatabases](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/ListAutonomousDatabases) |
| [GetAutonomousDataWarehouse](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDataWarehouse/GetAutonomousDataWarehouse) | [GetAutonomousDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/GetAutonomousDatabase) |
| [StartAutonomousDataWarehouse](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDataWarehouse/StartAutonomousDataWarehouse) | [StartAutonomousDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/StartAutonomousDatabase) |
| [StopAutonomousDataWarehouse](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDataWarehouse/StopAutonomousDataWarehouse) | [StopAutonomousDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/StopAutonomousDatabase) |
| [UpdateAutonomousDataWarehouse](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDataWarehouse/UpdateAutonomousDataWarehouse) | [UpdateAutonomousDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/UpdateAutonomousDatabase) |
| [GenerateAutonomousDataWarehouseWallet](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDataWarehouse/GenerateAutonomousDataWarehouseWallet) | [GenerateAutonomousDatabaseWallet](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/GenerateAutonomousDatabaseWallet) |
| [RestoreAutonomousDataWarehouse](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDataWarehouse/RestoreAutonomousDataWarehouses) | [RestoreAutonomousDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/RestoreAutonomousDatabase) |
| [DeleteAutonomousDataWarehouse](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDataWarehouse/DeleteAutonomousDataWarehouses) | [DeleteAutonomousDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/DeleteAutonomousDatabase) |
| [CreateAutonomousDataWarehouseBackup](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDataWarehouseBackup/CreateAutonomousDataWarehouseBackup) | [CreateAutonomousDatabaseBackup](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabaseBackup/CreateAutonomousDatabaseBackup) |
| [ListAutonomousDataWarehouseBackups](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDataWarehouseBackup/ListAutonomousDataWarehouseBackups) | [ListAutonomousDatabaseBackups](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabaseBackup/ListAutonomousDatabaseBackups) |
| [GetAutonomousDataWarehouseBackup](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDataWarehouseBackup/GetAutonomousDataWarehouseBackup) | [GetAutonomousDatabaseBackup](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabaseBackup/GetAutonomousDatabaseBackup) |

[Autonomous AI Database deprecated API fields and changed success response values (May 17, 2023)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change**: The following APIs have changes where the API or certain API fields are deprecated.

**Date Announced**: May 17, 2023.

**Date in Effect**: May 17, 2024.

**Details**: Prior to this service change, these APIs included the APIs or the API fields mentioned. After this service change, the mentioned APIs or APIs fields will be removed. This is applicable for Autonomous AI Database Serverless.

Support for these Autonomous AI Database API Fields will end on May 2, 2024. Oracle recommends that you migrate your scripts to discontinue the use of these Fields as soon as possible. Where provided, switch to use the replacement field or API.

**Deprecated Autonomous AI Database APIs:**

*   AutonomousDataWarehouse
*   AutonomousDataWarehouseSummary

**Deprecated Autonomous AI Database API Fields:**

*   CreateAutonomousDatabaseBase Deprecated API Fields:
    *   isDataGuardEnabled
    *   isLocalDataGuardEnabled

*   CreateRefreshableAutonomousDatabaseCloneDetails Deprecated API Fields:
    *   autoRefreshPolicy
    *   autoRefreshFrequencyInSeconds
    *   autoRefreshPointInSeconds
    *   timeOfAutoRefreshStart

*   UpdateAutonomousDatabaseDetails Deprecated API Fields:
    *   autoRefreshPolicy
    *   autoRefreshFrequencyInSeconds
    *   autoRefreshPointInSeconds
    *   timeOfAutoRefreshStart
    *   isDataGuardEnabled

*   AutonomousDatabaseSummary Deprecated API Fields:
    *   standbyDb
    *   dataguardRegionType
    *   timeDataGuardRoleChanged
    *   isDataGuardEnabled
    *   isLocalDataGuardEnabled
    *   serviceConsoleUrl

*   UpdateAutonomousDatabaseWalletDetails Deprecated API Field:
    *   shouldRotate

*   AutonomousDatabaseStandbySummary Deprecated API Field:
    *   timeDataGuardRoleChange

**Replacement for Deprecated APIs:**

| **Deprecated API** | **Replacement API** |
| --- | --- |
| CreateCrossRegionAutonomousDatabaseDataGuardDetails | CreateCrossRegionDisasterRecoveryDetails |
| AutonomousDataWarehouse | No replacement |
| AutonomousDataWarehouseSummary | No replacement |

**Replacement for Deprecated API Fields:**

| **Deprecated API Field** | **Replacement API Field** |
| --- | --- |
| UpdateAutonomousDatabaseDetails.isDataGuardEnabled | UpdateAutonomousDatabaseDetails.isLocalDataGuardEnabled |
| AutonomousDatabaseSummary.standbyDb | AutonomousDatabaseSummary.localStandbyDb |
| AutonomousDatabaseSummary.isDataGuardEnabled | AutonomousDatabaseSummary.localDisasterRecoveryType |
| AutonomousDatabaseStandbySummary.timeDataGuardRoleChange | AutonomousDatabaseStandbySummary.timeDisasterRecoveryRoleChanged |

**Changed Success Response Values for APIs:**

| **Autonomous Database API** | **Current Success Response Value** | **Updated Sucess Response Value** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| createAutonomousDatabase | 200 | 202 |
| updateAutonomousDatabase | 200 | 202 |
| restoreAutonomousDatabase | 200 | 202 |
| startAutonomousDatabase | 200 | 202 |
| restartAutonomousDatabase | 200 | 202 |
| stopAutonomousDatabase | 200 | 202 |
| failOverAutonomousDatabase | 200 | 202 |
| switchoverAutonomousDatabase | 200 | 202 |
| autonomousDatabaseManualRefresh | 200 | 202 |

**Does this impact me?**: If you have custom scripts or Terraform scripts that are referencing these APIs, or the mentioned fields, you should modify the scripts to account for these changes.

**What do I need to do?**: When using OCI SDKs and command line tools, you update your custom scripts to either remove use of the deprecated APIs or fields or use the replacements.

[Autonomous AI Database isMTLConnectionRequired API attribute: changed default values (Jul. 1, 2023)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change**: The default value of the `isMTLSConnectionRequired` attribute will change from `true` to `false` on July 1, 2023 in the following APIs:

*   [CreateAutonomousDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/CreateAutonomousDatabase)
    
*   [GetAutonomousDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/GetAutonomousDatabase)
    
*   [UpdateAutonomousDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/UpdateAutonomousDatabase)
    

**Date Announced**: February 7, 2023.

**Date in Effect**: July 1, 2023.

**Details**: Prior to this service change, the `isMTLSConnectionRequired` attribute default value was `true`. This is applicable for Autonomous AI Database Serverless.

**Does this impact me**? If you have custom scripts or Terraform scripts that are referencing the `CreateAutonomousDatabase`, `GetAutonomousDatabase`, or `UpdateAutonomousDatabase` APIs, you might want to modify the scripts to account for the changed default value of this attribute. However, should you choose not to make any changes to your scripts, the API calls containing this attribute will continue to work except that the default value will switch from "true" to "false".

**What do I need to do**? When using OCI SDKs and command line tools, you might choose to update your custom scripts to explicitly set the `isMTLSConnectionRequired` attribute to "true".

[Autonomous AI Database return values for APIs changed (Oct. 1, 2023)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change**: The return values for certain APIs changes, where return the value of `409 Incorrect State` sometimes, as appropriate, is changed to `409 Conflict`.

**Date Announced**: October 2023.

**Date in Effect**: October 2024.

**Details**: Before this service change, certain API calls fail with `409 Incorrect State` error code. After this service change, in certain cases as appropriate, the API calls will fail with error code `409 Conflict`.

Before this change, many APIs return `409 Incorrect State` when the Autonomous AI Database is Stopped or is Unavailable. For these states, according to the API guidelines the correct return is `409 Conflict`. For other Autonomous AI Database states, such as Starting, Stopping, and Provisioning, the current return of `409 Incorrect State` is correct and this will not change after this update.

The `409 Incorrect State` return should be used to indicate that retries are OK and that the resource will eventually reach the correct state, and the return `409 Conflict` indicates that the resource will not reach the correct state on its own and that retries should not be performed. This service change changes the error code value in these APIs for the cases where `409 Conflict` is representative of the known Autonomous AI Database state.

This service change applies to the following APIs:

*   [UpdateAutonomousDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/UpdateAutonomousDatabase)
    
*   [DeleteAutonomousDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/DeleteAutonomousDatabase)
    
*   [ChangeDisasterRecoveryConfiguration](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/ChangeDisasterRecoveryConfiguration)
    
*   [RotateAutonomousDatabaseEncryptionKey](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/RotateAutonomousDatabaseEncryptionKey)
    
*   [StartAutonomousDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/StartAutonomousDatabase)
    
*   [RestartAutonomousDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/RestartAutonomousDatabase)
    
*   [ShrinkAutonomousDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/ShrinkAutonomousDatabase)
    
*   [StopAutonomousDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/StopAutonomousDatabase)
    
*   [ConfigureAutonomousDatabaseVaultKey](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/ConfigureAutonomousDatabaseVaultKey)
    
*   [AutonomousDatabaseManualRefresh](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/AutonomousDatabaseManualRefresh)
    
*   [FailOverAutonomousDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/FailOverAutonomousDatabase)
    
*   [SwitchoverAutonomousDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/SwitchoverAutonomousDatabase)
    
*   [ChangeAutonomousDatabaseCompartment](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/ChangeAutonomousDatabaseCompartment)
    
*   [RegisterAutonomousDatabaseDataSafe](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/RegisterAutonomousDatabaseDataSafe)
    
*   [DeregisterAutonomousDatabaseDataSafe](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/DeregisterAutonomousDatabaseDataSafe)
    
*   [EnableAutonomousDatabaseOperationsInsights](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/EnableAutonomousDatabaseOperationsInsights)
    
*   [DisableAutonomousDatabaseOperationsInsights](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/DisableAutonomousDatabaseOperationsInsights)
    

**Does this impact me?**: If you have custom scripts or Terraform scripts that are handling the `409 Incorrect State` return from these APIs, you might want to modify the scripts to handle the `409 Conflict` return, as appropriate.

**What do I need to do?**: When using OCI SDKs and command line tools, you might choose to update your custom scripts.

[Autonomous AI Database isShared parameter for autonomousDatabaseCharacterSets deprecated (Oct. 1, 2023)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change**: The `isShared` parameter from the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure's [ListAutonomousDatabaseCharacterSets](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabaseCharacterSets/ListAutonomousDatabaseCharacterSets)
 is deprecated.

**Date Announced**: October 2023.

**Date in Effect**: October 2024.

**Details**: Before this service change, the optional `isShared` parameter can be used. This change introduces the optional `isDedicated` parameter, and the `isShared` parameter will be removed after October 2024.

**Does this impact me?**: If you have custom scripts or Terraform scripts that are referencing the [ListAutonomousDatabaseCharacterSets](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabaseCharacterSets/ListAutonomousDatabaseCharacterSets)
 API using the `isShared` parameter with the value TRUE, modify the scripts to replace this parameter with the `isDedicated` parameter with the value FALSE, and if you are referencing the [ListAutonomousDatabaseCharacterSets](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabaseCharacterSets/ListAutonomousDatabaseCharacterSets)
 API using the `isShared` parameter with the value FALSE, modify the scripts to replace this parameter with the `isDedicated` attribute with the value TRUE.

**What do I need to do?**: When using OCI SDKs and command line tools, update your custom scripts to replace the `isShared` parameter with the `isDedicated` parameter.

[Autonomous AI Database Serverless Manual Backup Deprecated (Feb. 15, 2024)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change**: On Autonomous AI Database Serverless the ability to take manual backups, which aren't long-term backups, is being deprecated.

Autonomous AI Database Serverless automatically backs up the database up to 60 days. Due to this change, on February 15, 2025 when you call the [CreateAutonomousDatabaseBackupDetails](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/datatypes/CreateAutonomousDatabaseBackupDetails)
 API with the `isLongTermBackup` attribute, the value must be set to `true`. The default value of the `isLongTermBackup` attribute will also change to `true`.

**Date Announced**: February 15, 2024.

**Date in Effect**: February 15, 2025.

**Details**: Prior to this service change, the attribute `isLongTermBackup` default value was `false`. After this service change, the only valid value for the `isLongTermBackup` attribute is `true`. This change is applicable for Autonomous AI Database Serverless.

**Does this impact me?**: If you have custom scripts or Terraform scripts that are referencing the [CreateAutonomousDatabaseBackupDetails](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/datatypes/CreateAutonomousDatabaseBackupDetails)
 API, you might want to modify the scripts to account for the changed default value of this attribute. However, if you choose not to make any changes to your scripts, the API calls containing this attribute will continue to work except that the default value will switch from `false` to `true`.

**What do I need to do?**: When using OCI SDKs and command line tools, update your custom scripts to explicitly set the `isLongTermBackup` attribute to `true`.

[Autonomous AI Database Serverless Oracle Machine Learning Notebooks Classic deprecated (Jun. 11, 2024)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Oracle Machine Learning Notebooks Classic on Autonomous AI Database Serverless, which is the original notebook environment and replaced by an enhanced notebook interface, is deprecated.

The new OML Notebooks interface provides a new look and feel, along with new features: faster notebook loading and access; rich data visualizations, paragraph comments and runtime dependencies; Zeppelin and Jupyter layouts; on-page notebook versioning, history viewing, and version comparison; GPU support for Python paragraphs; along with the ability to use third-party R and Python packages via conda environments.

**Date Announced:** June 11, 2024, in the Oracle Machine Learning Notebooks home page "Importance changes" banner.

**Date in Effect:** December 31, 2024.

**Details:** Aligned with the banner notice and prior to feature removal, the following incremental changes will be rolled out:

Phase 1 - April 17, 2023

*   [Oracle Machine Learning Notebooks releases Notebooks Early Adopter interface](https://blogs.oracle.com/machinelearning/post/announcing-next-generation-to-oml-notebooks-on-oracle-autonomous-database)
     and provides the ability to convert Classic notebooks to note notebooks conveniently in the notebooks listing page.

Phase 2 - October 29, 2024

*   On the Notebook Classic listing page, users will no longer be able to create Classic notebooks, save them as templates, or select them for job scheduling.
*   Existing Classic notebooks can be opened in read-only mode.
*   Users can convert Classic notebooks to the new format using the "Copy to OML Notebooks" button on the listing page.

Phase 3 - December 31, 2024

*   Classic notebooks will no longer be available to users.
*   The ADMIN user can access Classic notebooks in read-only mode and convert them to the new format.
*   Jobs that still use Classic notebooks will be disabled with a status shown as “Disabled”. Any associated job logs will not be accessible. Users will be able to delete jobs using Classic notebooks only.

Phase 4 -June 4, 2025

*   The ADMIN user will no longer have access to Classic notebooks.
*   Any remaining Classic notebooks will be deleted.

**Does this impact me?** This affects only users who have Classic notebooks, Classic template notebooks, or jobs that specify Classic notebooks. Per the schedule above, users will lose access to these notebooks and existing jobs using Classic notebooks will produce an error if they are run or are scheduled on a recurring schedule.

**What do I need to do?** If you have Classic notebooks or Classic template notebooks (personal or shared) that you wish to keep, you must convert these to the new format. If you have jobs that rely on Classic notebooks, these jobs must either be updated with a new notebook or recreated with a new notebook.

[Autonomous AI Database on Dedicated Exadata infrastructure and Cloud@Customer deprecated Autonomous Data Guard APIs (Mar. 4, 2025)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Support for the Autonomous Container Database Data Guard Association and Autonomous Database Data Guard Association APIs listed in this service change announcement ends on March 4, 2026.

**Date Announced:** March 4, 2025.

**Date in Effect:** March 4, 2026.

**Details:** We recommend that you migrate database workloads using the APIs in this announcement to the listed replacement APIs as soon as possible.

| [SwitchoverAutonomousContainerDatabaseDataguardAssociation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/20160918/AutonomousContainerDatabaseDataguardAssociation/SwitchoverAutonomousContainerDatabaseDataguardAssociation) | [SwitchoverAutonomousContainerDatabaseDataguard](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/20160918/AutonomousContainerDatabaseDataguardAssociation/SwitchoverAutonomousContainerDatabaseDataguardAssociation) |

| [GetAutonomousDatabaseDataguardAssociation](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/20160918/AutonomousDatabaseDataguardAssociation/GetAutonomousDatabaseDataguardAssociation) | [GetAutonomousContainerDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/20160918/AutonomousContainerDatabase/GetAutonomousContainerDatabase) |
| [ListAutonomousDatabaseDataguardAssociations](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/20160918/AutonomousDatabaseDataguardAssociation/ListAutonomousDatabaseDataguardAssociations) | [GetAutonomousContainerDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/20160918/AutonomousContainerDatabase/GetAutonomousContainerDatabase) |

[Autonomous Database API cpuCoreCount field is deprecated (May 28, 2025)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

The `cpuCoreCount` field is deprecated for the Autonomous Database APIs detailed in this service change announcement.

Prior to this service change, Autonomous Database APIs in this service change announcement included `cpuCoreCount` as a field. After this service change is implemented, the `cpuCoreCount` field will be removed. We recommend that users update their scripts and automation to stop the use of this field as soon as possible. Where provided, users should switch to use the ECPU Compute model and `computeCount`. Support for this Autonomous Database API field ends on May 28, 2026.

Affected APIs:

*   [CreateAutonomousDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabase/CreateAutonomousDatabase)
    
*   [UpdateAutonomousDatabaseDetails](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/datatypes/UpdateAutonomousDatabaseDetails)
    
*   [AutonomousDatabaseSummary](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/AutonomousDatabaseSummary)
    

### Exadata Services

[Exadata Database Service on Dedicated Infrastructure Deprecated APIs (Nov. 15, 2020)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

Oracle Cloud Infrastructure's Exadata DB system APIs were deprecated on November 15, 2020.

**Important!:** No new systems can be provisioned with the old DB system resource model/APIs after May 15th, 2021. Support for the old DB system resource model/APIs on existing systems will end on November 15th, 2021. Oracle recommends that you migrate your Exadata Database Service on Dedicated Infrastructure instances to the new resource model APIs as soon as possible. Converting to the new resource model does not involve any system downtime.

| Unsupported API | Replacement APIs |
| --- | --- |
| [LaunchDbSystem](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/DbSystem/LaunchDbSystem)<br> _(deprecated for Exadata systems only)_ | [CreateCloudExadataInfrastructure](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/CloudExadataInfrastructure/CreateCloudExadataInfrastructure/)<br> and [CreateCloudVmCluster](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/CloudVmCluster/CreateCloudVmCluster/) |
| [ListDbSystems](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/DbSystem/ListDbSystems)<br> _(deprecated for Exadata systems only)_ | [ListCloudExadataInfrastructures](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/CloudExadataInfrastructure/ListCloudExadataInfrastructures/)<br> and [ListCloudVmClusters](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/CloudVmCluster/ListCloudVmClusters/) |
| [GetDbSystem](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/DbSystem/GetDbSystem)<br> _(deprecated for Exadata systems only)_ | [GetCloudExadataInfrastructure](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/CloudExadataInfrastructure/GetCloudExadataInfrastructure/)<br> and [GetCloudVmCluster](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/CloudVmCluster/GetCloudVmCluster/) |
| [ChangeDbSystemCompartment](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/DbSystem/ChangeDbSystemCompartment)<br> _(deprecated for Exadata systems only)_ | [ChangeCloudExadataInfrastructureCompartment](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/CloudExadataInfrastructure/ChangeCloudExadataInfrastructureCompartment/)<br> and [ChangeCloudVmClusterCompartment](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/CloudVmCluster/ChangeCloudVmClusterCompartment/) |
| [UpdateDbSystem](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/DbSystem/UpdateDbSystem)<br> _(deprecated for Exadata systems only)_ | [UpdateCloudExadataInfrastructure](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/CloudExadataInfrastructure/UpdateCloudExadataInfrastructure/)<br> and [UpdateCloudVmCluster](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/CloudVmCluster/UpdateCloudVmCluster/) |
| [GetExadataIormConfig](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/DbSystem/GetExadataIormConfig)<br> _(deprecated for Exadata systems only)_ | [GetCloudVmClusterIormConfig](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/CloudVmCluster/GetCloudVmClusterIormConfig/) |
| [UpdateExadataIormConfig](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/DbSystem/UpdateExadataIormConfig)<br> _(Exadata systems only)_ | [UpdateCloudVmClusterIormConfig](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/CloudVmCluster/UpdateCloudVmClusterIormConfig/) |
| [TerminateDbSystem](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/DbSystem/TerminateDbSystem)<br> _(deprecated for Exadata systems only)_ | [DeleteCloudExadataInfrastructure](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/CloudExadataInfrastructure/DeleteCloudExadataInfrastructure/)<br> and [DeleteCloudVmCluster](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/CloudVmCluster/DeleteCloudVmCluster/) |

[Database workload API attribute for Base Database, Exadata Database Service on Dedicated Infrastructure and Exadata Database Service on Cloud@Customer deprecated (Nov. 1, 2022)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The optional `dbWorkload` attribute from the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure's [CreateDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/datatypes/CreateDatabaseDetails)
 API is deprecated.

**Date Announced:** November 2022.

**Date in Effect:** November 2023.

**Details:** Prior to this service change, the `dbWorkload` attribute can be used to choose between either the OLTP (Online Transaction Processing) or the Data Warehouse (Analytic) workload, and is used internally to determine memory settings based on the database workload. After this service change, the `dbWorkload` attribute is treated as a "no-op" (no operation), which means that while API calls containing the deprecated attribute will not fail, the passed value will be ignored and the system will instead use a default value internally. This is applicable for Exadata Database Service on Dedicated Infrastructure, Exadata Database Service on Cloud@Customer, and Base Database Service.

**Does this impact me?** If you have custom scripts or Terraform scripts that are referencing the CreateDatabase API and are explicitly using the `dbWorkload` attribute, you might want to modify the scripts to remove this attribute. However, should you choose not to make any changes to your scripts, the API calls containing this attribute will continue to work except that the value passed for the `dbWorkload` attribute will not be considered.

**What do I need to do?** When using [OCI SDKs](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm#Software_Development_Kits_and_Command_Line_Interface)
 and [command line tools](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/)
, you might choose to update your custom scripts to exclude the `dbWorkload` attribute. After November 2023, if you pass a value to the `dbWorkload` attribute, it will be ignored.

### Oracle Base Database Service

[Database workload API attribute for Base Database, Exadata Database Service on Dedicated Infrastructure and Exadata Database Service on Cloud@Customer deprecated (Nov. 1, 2022)](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The optional `dbWorkload` attribute from the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure's [CreateDatabase](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database/latest/datatypes/CreateDatabaseDetails)
 API is deprecated.

**Date Announced:** November 2022.

**Date in Effect:** November 2023.

**Details:** Prior to this service change, the `dbWorkload` attribute can be used to choose between either the OLTP (Online Transaction Processing) or the Data Warehouse (Analytic) workload, and is used internally to determine memory settings based on the database workload. After this service change, the `dbWorkload` attribute is treated as a "no-op" (no operation), which means that while API calls containing the deprecated attribute will not fail, the passed value will be ignored and the system will instead use a default value internally. This is applicable for Exadata Database Service on Dedicated Infrastructure, Exadata Database Service on Cloud@Customer, and Base Database Service.

**Does this impact me?** If you have custom scripts or Terraform scripts that are referencing the CreateDatabase API and are explicitly using the `dbWorkload` attribute, you might want to modify the scripts to remove this attribute. However, should you choose not to make any changes to your scripts, the API calls containing this attribute will continue to work except that the value passed for the `dbWorkload` attribute will not be considered.

**What do I need to do?** When using [OCI SDKs](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/API/Concepts/sdks.htm#Software_Development_Kits_and_Command_Line_Interface)
 and [command line tools](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/tools/oci-cli/latest/oci_cli_docs/)
, you might choose to update your custom scripts to exclude the `dbWorkload` attribute. After November 2023, if you pass a value to the `dbWorkload` attribute, it will be ignored.

Database Management
-------------------

[Database Management for MySQL HeatWave End of Life](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Database Management for MySQL HeatWave End of Life

**Date Announced:** January 29, 2026

**Date in Effect:** January 29, 2027

**Details:** MySQL HeatWave support within the OCI Database Management service has been deprecated as of January 29, 2026, and new MySQL HeatWave DB systems can no longer be enabled, except for Database Management basic monitoring. Additionally, this MySQL functionality will be obsoleted as of January 29, 2027.

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you currently use Database Management for MySQL HeatWave to monitor MySQL DB systems.

**What do I need to do?**

*   Plan and execute a transition to an alternative monitoring solution before the effective date to avoid disruption.
*   Evaluate and select a replacement that fits your business and technical requirements. Third‑party options such as Prometheus and Grafana dashboards are commonly used for MySQL monitoring. Verify security and compliance with your organization's requirements and Oracle's internal guidelines before adoption.
*   For assistance or additional information, submit a Service Request through My Oracle Support.

[Database Management SQL Performance Watch End of Life](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change**: Database Management SQL Performance Watch End of Life

**Date Announced**: January 29, 2026

**Date in Effect**: January 29, 2027

**Details**: SQL Performance Watch support within the OCI Database Management service has been deprecated as of January 29, 2026. As of this date, no new databases can be enabled with SQL Performance Watch. Additionally, SQL Performance Watch will be obsoleted as of January 29, 2027.

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you currently use Database Management SQL Performance Watch functionality for your databases.

**What do I need to do?** To ensure continued SQL performance monitoring and analysis, we recommend evaluating SQL Performance Analyzer in Oracle Enterprise Manager as an alternative. SQL Performance Analyzer offers deep-dive analytics, root cause diagnostics, and comprehensive insights for SQL performance troubleshooting and optimization. For assistance or additional information, submit a Service Request through My Oracle Support.

[OCI Vulnerability Detection and Patching End of Life](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change**: OCI Vulnerability Detection and Patching End of Life

**Date Announced**: January 29, 2026

**Date in Effect:** January 29, 2027

**Details**: OCI Vulnerability Detection and Patching support within the OCI Database Management service has been deprecated as of January 29, 2026. As of this date, no new databases can be enabled with the OCI Vulnerability Detection and Patching service. Additionally, this service will be obsoleted as of January 29, 2027.

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you currently use the OCI Vulnerability Detection and Patching service within OCI Database Management to identify vulnerabilities and manage database patching in your environments.

**What do I need to do?** Plan and execute a transition to Fleet Maintenance in Oracle Enterprise Manager as an alternative solution for OCI Vulnerability Detection and Patching before January 29, 2027, to avoid service disruption. Fleet Maintenance enables centralized, automated patching and maintenance of Oracle database fleets across environments. For assistance or additional information, submit a Service Request through My Oracle Support.

Database Migration
------------------

[Deprecated Create Migration Option](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change**: The **Create migration** option in Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Database Migration Service is planned to be deprecated from April 7, 2027 onwards.

**Date Announced**: April 7, 2026

**Date in Effect:** April 7, 2027

**Details**: Starting from April 7, 2026, you should create all the new migrations through an assessment. The **Create migration** option is planned to be removed after one year of this announcement and you will first need to complete an assessment to create a migration.

**Does this impact me?** If you're an existing customer using the OCI Console and the version of the source Oracle Database is 19c (19.23) or above, then switch to the **Create assessment** functionality. However, if your source database versions are older than Oracle Database 19c (19.23), then you can continue to use the existing [Create migration](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/database-migration/dmsus/create-migrations.html)
 flow.

**What do I need to do?** Use the **Create assessment** functionality now to prepare for this change and to ensure a smooth transition. See [Create assessments](https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/paas/database-migration/dmsus/create-assessments.html)
 for more details.

[Deprecated Database Migration APIs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Oracle Cloud Infrastructure's Database Migration API version `201210929` is deprecated from June 21, 2024 onwards.

**Date Announced:** July 2, 2024

**Date in Effect:** June 21, 2024

**Details:**

Starting from June 21, 2024, the older version of Database Migration API, version `201210929` is deprecated. From June 21, 2025, onwards, the deprecated API is no longer available and it's replaced with a new API version `20230518` that provides enhanced features and support for future releases.

**Does this impact me?**

*   If you're a new customer and have never used our service before, this change doesn't impact you. (or)
*   If you're an existing customer using the OCI Console for managing Database Migration resources, and you have migrations and connections created before this change, then, you must switch to the new API by creating new migrations and connections. However, this doesn't impact the existing migration that are in progress and you can delete the older migrations and connections.
*   If you're using an older version of the public SDK or the Terraform provider, you can no longer perform certain operations on the resources using the OCI Console. However, you can continue to use the deprecated APIs using the older SDKs until the support is removed from the service. For more information, you can contact Support.

**What do I need to do?**

If you're using an older version of the SDK or the Terraform provider, you must plan to upgrade it to the new SDK or Terraform provider at the earliest.

**List of deprecated and replacement APIs**

| Deprecated API [20210929](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database-migration/20210929) | Replacement API [20230518](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#/en/database-migration/latest/) |

Developer Tools
---------------

[OCI Java SDK Version 2 Is Deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** OCI Java SDK version 2 is being deprecated.

**Date Announced:** April 6, 2023

**Date in Effect:** June 30, 2023

**Details:** Oracle has recently released Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Java SDK version 3. OCI Java SDK version 3 is a major SDK release, and we recommend that you upgrade to this latest version. We will continue to fully support OCI Java SDK version 2 until the end of June 2023. During this time, both OCI Java SDK versions 2 and 3 will receive regular updates to add support for new service APIs, critical bug fixes and security patches, and documentation modifications.

**Does this impact me?** After June 30, 2023, OCI Java SDK version 2 will no longer receive updates to add support for new regions, new services, or features in existing services, unless otherwise specified. For older versions of the OCI Java SDK version 2 that are less than 12 months old, upon [request](https://support.oracle.com/portal/)
, OCI will backport critical bug fixes and security issues only.

**What do I need to do?** Upgrade to OCI Java SDK version 3.

DevOps
------

[Deprecated DevOps APIs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Oracle Cloud Infrastructure's DevOps APIs (two APIs) of version `20210630` were deprecated on March 29, 2022.

**Date Announced:** March 29, 2022

**Date in Effect:** March 29, 2023

**Details:** Starting March 29, 2022, two DevOps APIs of version `20210630` were deprecated. From March 29, 2023, onwards the deprecated APIs will be unavailable.

| Deprecated API | Replacement API |
| --- | --- |
| [GetRepositoryFileLines](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/devops/latest/Repository/GetRepositoryFileLines) | [GetRepoFileLines](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/devops/20210630/Repository/GetRepoFileLines) |
| [GetFileDiff](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/devops/20210630/Repository/GetFileDiff) | [GetRepoFileDiff](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/api/#%23/en/devops/20210630/Repository/GetRepoFileDiff) |

[DevOps Ends Support for Oracle Linux 7](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** OCI DevOps ends support for Oracle Linux 7.

**Date Announced:** August 27, 2025

**Date in Effect:** August 28, 2026

**Details:** Effective August 28, 2026, the support for Oracle Linux 7 comes to an end, after which users can't create or run builds on OL7-based build runners. To ensure a smooth transition, we advise migrating to Oracle Linux 8 before October 12, 2025 to avoid latency in Oracle Linux 7-based build runner launches. To ensure a seamless transition and best build performance, update the pipeline configurations to use Oracle Linux 8.

Event Hub
---------

[Event Hub service deprecated](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The Event Hub service is deprecated.

**Date Announced:** April 29, 2022

**Date in Effect:** May 31, 2023

**Details:** Effective May 31, 2023, the Oracle Event Hub service will reach end of life (EOL). Prior to the EOL date, we recommend that you migrate your data streams from Event Hub to [Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Streaming](https://www.oracle.com/cloud/cloud-native/streaming/?elqat=2&elqaid=96348&elqTrackId=516d7366256f4363b1733960f1cdf191)
 .

**Does this impact me?** If you use Event Hub service to create Kafka Clusters and/or Event Hub topics, you will no longer be able to after May 31, 2023. Previously created clusters will continue to operate in your tenancy without changes.

**What do I need to do?** All Event Hub customers can now use [Streaming](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Streaming/home.htm)
 to move data using its tight integrations with Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI), Database, GoldenGate, and Integration Cloud. The service uses a [Kafka Connect harness](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/Streaming/Tasks/managingkafkaconnectharnesses.htm)
 to provide out-of-the-box integrations for hundreds of third-party products across categories such as DevOps, databases, big data, and SaaS applications.

File Storage
------------

[New service limit introduced for number of file systems attached to a snapshot policy](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** A maximum of 100 file systems may be attached to any particular snapshot policy.

**Date Announced:** August 5, 2023

**Date in Effect:** August 7, 2023

**Details:** Starting August 7, 2023, a new service limit will be introduced to limit the total number of file systems attached to a snapshot policy. This change will allow up to 100 file systems to be attached per snapshot policy per tenant per availability domain.

**Does this impact me?** If you're planning to attach more than 100 file systems to any particular snapshot policy, then you won't be able to do so after August 7, 2023. Any existing tenants who have more than 100 file systems attached per snapshot policy before August 7, 2023 will be given an exception. No exceptions will be given after August 7, 2023.

**What do I need to do?** If you need to attach more than 100 file systems to a snapshot policy, then create a second snapshot policy or use any other existing snapshot policy. You can create 100 snapshot policy per tenant per availability domain. You can still generate policy-based snapshots for file systems, but you might have to use more than one snapshot policy.

[New IAM policy required to grant file systems permissions to use custom encryption keys](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** File Storage file systems using customer-managed encryption keys need new IAM policies.

**Date Announced:** May 29, 2024

**Date in Effect:** May 29, 2024

**Details:** On May 29, 2024, File Storage introduces a new method for granting file systems permission to use custom encryption keys. Before this change, policies to grant file systems permissions to use custom encryption keys used the File Storage service user. For example:

    Allow service <file_storage_service_user> to use keys

After this change, File Storage resources are granted permission to use custom keys. For more information, see [Encrypting a File System](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/File/Tasks/encrypt-file-system.htm)
.

**Does this impact me?** Before May 29, 2024, File Storage file systems using customer-managed Vault encryption keys instead of Oracle-managed keys used the File Storage service user in required IAM policies.

The service user won't have access to customer-managed keys in the future.

**What do I need to do?** Create new IAM policies that lets File Storage encrypt and decrypt file systems using resource principals. Ensure file systems use resource principal policies instead of service principal policies.

For more information, see [Encrypting a File System](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/File/Tasks/encrypt-file-system.htm)
 and [Verifying Resource Principal Access to Encryption Keys](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/Content/File/Tasks/verify-encryption-key-principal.htm)
.

Fleet Application Management
----------------------------

[Fleet Application Management End of Life](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** OCI Fleet Application Management End of Life

**Date Announced:** May 6, 2026

**Date in Effect:** May 1, 2027

**Details:** OCI Fleet Application Management is deprecated as of May 6, 2026. If you are an existing OCI Fleet Application Management customer, you can continue to use the service in your existing tenancies and regions until the last date of service, May 1, 2027. For alternative cloud-based monitoring solutions, we recommend the following options:

*   Use Java Management Service (JMS) for Java workloads.
*   Use Database Management service to monitor all Oracle database and Exadata resources.
*   To monitor other resource types, select the appropriate OCI Observability & Management services.

**Does this impact me?** Yes, if you currently use OCI Fleet Application Management.

**What do I need to do**:

*   Plan and implement a transition to an alternative lifecycle management and resource management solution before the end-of-service date to prevent disruption.
*   Evaluate and select a replacement that meets your business and technical requirements. For cloud-based monitoring solutions, select Java Management Service (JMS) for Java workloads or OCI Database Management for monitoring Oracle database and Exadata resources. To monitor other resource types, use the applicable OCI Observability and Management services.
*   For assistance or additional information, submit a Service Request through My Oracle Support.

[Deprecated Fleet Application Management APIs](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** Oracle Cloud Infrastructure's Fleet Application Management APIs of version `20230831` were deprecated on May 14, 2025.

**Date Announced:** May 14, 2025

**Date in Effect:** May 14, 2025

**Details:** Starting May 14, 2025, Fleet Application Management APIs of version 20230831 was deprecated. From May 14, 2025, onwards the deprecated APIs will be unavailable. The Update/Delete/Get/List Operations on any resources created by deprecated APIs will be supported by the new APIs.

Full Stack Disaster Recovery
----------------------------

[Deprecated Member Type](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The `COMPUTE_INSTANCE` member type in `DrProtectionGroupMemberType` is getting deprecated and will no longer be supported.

**Date Announced:** October 31, 2023

**Date in Effect:** October 31, 2024.

**Details:** The member type `COMPUTE_INSTANCE` is getting deprecated and will be replaced by the following alternate member types:

*   `COMPUTE_INSTANCE_MOVABLE`: Used for compute instances that move during DR operations.
*   `COMPUTE_INSTANCE_NON_MOVABLE`: Used for compute instances that do not move during DR operations.

Migrate to one of the new instance types before the effective deprecation date.

**Does this impact me?** If you use the `COMPUTE_INSTANCE` member type in your DR configuration, this change affects you. Make sure that you migrate to one of the new instance types before the effective deprecation date.

**What do I need to do?** To migrate from an existing `COMPUTE_INSTANCE` to one of the new instance types, follow [these instructions](https://docs.oracle.com/iaas/disaster-recovery/doc/migrating-to-new-compute-instance.html)
.

[Deprecated blockVolumeOperations Property](https://docs.oracle.com/en-us/iaas/Content/servicechanges.htm#)

**Service Change:** The blockVolumeOperations property for a Non Movable Compute Instance is being deprecated and will no longer be supported.

**Date Announced:** May 20, 2025

**Date in Effect:** May 20, 2026

**Details:** The `blockVolumeOperations` property in `DrProtectionGroupMemberComputeInstanceNonMovable`, `CreateDrProtectionGroupMemberComputeInstanceNonMovableDetails`, and `UpdateDrProtectionGroupMemberComputeInstanceNonMovableDetails` data types are deprecated.
